title: Ground Floor Team Member
description: We're currently recruiting in our Dublin City Centre (Temple Bar) Premier Inn. Working 10 hours per week, paying up to €15\.25 per hour. Front of House – Dublin City Centre (Temple Bar) Premier Inn Come and be a Front of House Team Member at Premier Inn in Dublin City Centre (Temple Bar). As the friendly face of our hotel, use your fun personality and your customer service skills to welcome guests. Help them to rest easy from the moment they check in – be it making a cocktail, serving a meal or joining up with the housekeeping team to get rooms perfect. Immediate start, no experience needed. **PAY RATE:** Up to €15\.25 per hour **CONTRACT TYPE:** Fixed Term until 24/09/2026 **HOURS:** 10 hours a week **LOCATION:** Premier Inn Dublin City Centre (Temple Bar) 51 Stephen Street Lower, Dublin, DO2 N7Y3 **Why you’ll love it here:** **Training and support:** We’ve got a warm welcome and simple administration system to use that will make your job easier from the start PLUS the opportunities to give you a clear career path within our business **Whitbread Benefits card:** Enjoy up to 60% off Premier Inn rooms and 25% off meals in all Whitbread Restaurants **Pension and saving schemes:** Company pension and save as you earn scheme **Discounts:** Get money off shops, your utility bills, travel, cinema trips, supermarkets and more **Have breakfast on us:** Enjoy a complimentary Premier Inn breakfast for 2 when you join us **What you’ll do:** Join our team at Dublin City Centre (Temple Bar) Premier Inn as a Front of House Team Member, and use your passion for customer service to its full potential. Be part of the UK’s leading hospitality business, Whitbread.

title: HR Generalist - 1+ Years Experience
description: **HR Generalist** My client, a high growth financial services firm based in Dublin, has partnered exclusively with re:work to recruit a Junior HR generalist. This is a hands\-on, people\-facing role reporting directly to the Head of HR and sitting at the centre of a people\-first business that has doubled in size over the past year. **What Is On Offer** * Salary: €40,000 \- €45,000 depending on experience * 10% performance bonus * Hybrid working: 4 days from home, 1 day in office per week * 25 days annual leave * Employer pension contribution * Income protection and death in service **Role Summary** This is a broad generalist role covering the full employee lifecycle, with a particular focus on recruitment, onboarding, HR administration, and culture and engagement. The successful candidate will support the Head of HR in keeping day\-to\-day operations running smoothly, while contributing to the continued development of an award\-winning employee experience. It suits someone early in their HR career who is energetic, organised, and genuinely passionate about people. **About the Company and HR Team** Our client is a well\-established and rapidly scaling financial business, with significant further growth planned. Their people function has been built from the ground up by a highly regarded Head of HR. The culture is high\-trust, flexible, and employee\-centric. This is the first dedicated HR hire into the business, so there is real scope to grow alongside the company as it scales. **What You Will Be Doing** * Supporting end\-to\-end recruitment activity including scheduling, candidate communications, and coordinating a smooth onboarding experience for every new hire * Maintaining employee records and managing the HRIS system (HR Locker) with accuracy and care * Acting as a first point of contact for employee queries, providing clear, people\-focused support * Contributing to culture and engagement initiatives that underpin the company's standout workplace reputation * Coordinating company\-wide events and off\-sites, including two annual events held outside the office * Supporting day\-to\-day office management including suppliers and the workplace environment **What We Are Looking For** * At least 1 year of experience in an HR or people support role * High energy, proactive, and genuinely passionate about people and culture * Comfortable working at pace in a small, fast\-moving team where no two days look the same * Strong organisational skills and sharp attention to detail * A natural communicator who builds relationships easily at all levels * Working towards or holding an HR qualification; CIPD membership is a plus **How They Work** This is a hybrid role with one fixed day in the Dublin city centre office each week. The company operates on a high\-trust, flexible model and genuinely lives its work\-life balance values from the top down. There is zero expectation of late nights or weekend availability. **How to Apply** Please ensure your CV is updated before applying. Due to the high volume of applications, we are unable to accept submissions via email or LinkedIn messages. This role is being managed by Alexandra Rickard, a specialist HR Recruiter across Dublin and the broader Irish market. Whether it's a single key hire or building an entire People function, if your company is hiring, feel free to connect on LinkedIn **: Alexandra Rickard \| HR Recruiter.**

A valid Full\-time work permit is mandatory for us to proceed with your application.** **Kindly read carefully the paragraph in red before applying to the .** Our global policy 500 related to internal transfers via Careers Marketplace has recently been updated. **If you apply for an internal job at a higher level and are selected to join the team, you will be offered the job at your current level (no change in the level or salary). You will be considered for promotion at the next eligible promotion period.** **Although you won’t be promoted at the time you transfer, you still have the opportunity to move into the new , demonstrate your abilities and expand your skills — which will best position for promotion at the next eligible promotion period.** DESCRIPTION: This position is responsible for assisting with preparing, processing, filing and follow up stages of Accenture s Client HR procedures including, Exits, Transfers, Leaves, Right to work, Onboarding, as well as other processes. *Key Responsibilities* * Handle inbound employee cross functional teams inquiries. Providing Customer Service to Client s employees, using Salesforce CRM. * Preparing, processing and or updating employee s records related to transferring, Leaves, and terminating as well as other HR related processes in Workday, Salesforce and other client s systems. * Explaining Client s HR policies, procedures, related to transferring, Leaves, and terminating, to new and existing employees. * Maintaining computer system by updating and entering data. * Compiling reports and spreadsheets and preparing spreadsheets * Assisting with exist, Leaves and Transfers meetings with Client s employees * Attend team meetings and trainings as needed * Work on miscellaneous projects as instructed. * Knowledge, skills abilities * Candidates must be able to demonstrate a number of proficiencies that are essential to the position, which include * Must be adept at problem solving, including being able to identify issues and resolve programs in a timely manner * Must possess strong interpersonal skills * Must be able to communicate clearly, both written and orally, as to connect with employees, members of the Client s HR management team, and in group presentations and meetings * Must be able to optimally read and interpret information, present numerical data in an inventive manner, and expertly capture and analyse information * Must be able to prioritize and plan work activities as to use time efficiently * Must be organized, accurate, detailed, and able to supervise work for quality * Must be conscientious, able to follow instructions, respond to management direction, and must be able to improve performance through management feedback. ***NCLUSION AND DIVERSITY*** ***We are a global collective of diverse talents. Innovators improving the way the world works and lives. We provide an accessible, inclusive, open and welcoming workplace that helps everyone make the most of their professional potential. Discover how we are accelerating equality for all in the workplace.*** ***Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Interactive, Technology and Operations services — all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. Our 674,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities. title: Human Resources Generalist
description: **Role** **Tennis Ireland is looking for a HR Generalist** to join the Tennis Ireland Operations team based in the Sport Ireland Campus in Blanchardstown, Dublin. The function of the role is to lead in the provision of the full range of HR activities. The successful candidate should be interested in joining a fast\-paced environment, trusted to deliver people centric, progressive, and tailored HR solutions that are aligned to the Tennis Ireland strategy. The successful candidate will report to the Operations Manager. **Key Duties and Responsibilities** **HR Generalist Responsibilities:** **· People Strategy \& Culture:** Lead the Tennis Ireland HR People Strategy and support the leadership team on HR strategic initiatives. · **Recruitment:** Handle the end\-to\-end recruitment process including job specification creation, posting job advertisements, screening candidates, creating scoring matrices and scheduling and conducting interviews. · **Employee Onboarding:** Complete employee reference checks and ensure that the relevant onboarding documentation is obtained including safeguarding training and garda vetting compliance. Complete the employee induction and coordinate IT system and payroll set\-up to ensure a seamless integration to the organisation. Prepare and issue contracts. · **Employee Records:** Maintain accurate and up to date employee records on our HR management system (HR Locker). · **Training Needs Analysis:** Identify training needs, research and coordinate training delivery and maintain training records. · **Leavers Process:** Manage the full leaver process, including conducting exit interviews and providing necessary final payroll adjustments. · **Succession Planning:** Work closely with the leadership team to identify and develop successors creating a talent pipeline. · **Performance Management:** Support the leadership team with the performance management processes providing necessary training, coaching and guidance as required. Monitor the employee probation and performance review process and support managers in delivering meaningful, values and behaviour\-based feedback and employee development planning. · **HR Policies \& Procedures:** Stay abreast of industry best practices and changing legislation and update policies and procedures accordingly. Provide HR support and advice on policies and procedures to all employees. · **Reporting:** Provide regular HR reports / presentations to the CEO, leadership team and the HR Remuneration Sub Committee. Coordinate CEO reporting requirements as and when required ensuring timely delivery of report submissions. · **Employee Engagement:** Develop and implement employee engagement strategies to create a strong workplace culture. Manage and assess employee satisfaction through annual employee engagement surveys. Lead and coordinate employee well\-being events. Design and coordinate the staff day agenda, presentation and format working in close alignment with the CEO and Operations Manager. Maximise employee interaction and engagement throughout the session providing opportunity for networking, brainstorming and meaningful information cascade. · **Tennis Ireland Values \& Behaviours:** Champion the organisation values (integrity, inclusiveness, community, excellence) and behaviours (leadership, respect, nurture, standards) ensuring that they are embedded across recruitment, performance management, learning and development and employee engagement activities. · Ad hoc duties or project work as the role evolves. **Data Protection Officer:** **· Law \& Regulation Understanding:** Understand data protection laws and regulations and ensure Tennis Ireland adheres to same (training will be provided). · **Policies \& Procedures:** Implement policies, procedures and best practices that align with compliance requirements and conduct regular audits to identify risks and implement corrective actions. Develop and implement data governing policies including data retention, processing and disposal practices. · **Conduct Assessments:** Conduct assessments i.e. Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) to evaluate risks associated with data processing activities. Document security and privacy gaps and address accordingly. · **Data Protection Liaison:** Serve as the main liaison between Tennis Ireland and regulatory authorities / Legal Counsel and handle all enquiries and complaints from individuals regarding their data rights. · **Reporting:** Provide data protection reports / updates to the Board as and when required. **Deputy Company Secretary Responsibilities:** **· Provide deputy company secretarial support, including board pack preparation, minute‑taking, action tracking, and attending board meetings as required.** **About Tennis Ireland** **Founded in 1908, Tennis Ireland serves as the National Governing Body for the sport of tennis in Ireland. It comprises 192 affiliated clubs and a growing community of over 100,000 players. Tennis Ireland has a dual remit to sustain and grow our large tennis community, and to provide the necessary pathways and performance ecosystem for young players who aspire to play on the professional tennis tour. Tennis Ireland is at a key growth and inflection point, advancing its strategic ambitions to build resilience, drive inclusivity, and ensure operational excellence across the sport.** **Tennis Ireland stages Pro Tour events including the Irish Open Championships and selects teams to participate in the Billie Jean King Cup, Davis Cup and other international events at Junior, Senior and Masters level. Tennis Ireland also supports the delivery of a wide range of competitive, educational and development tennis programmes at local, provincial and national levels all across the island of Ireland. Hence our purpose is to give everyone the game of their life.** **Experience Required** · Minimum 5 years' relevant HR Generalist experience. · Thorough knowledge of current employment legislation and its application. · Excellent Microsoft Office skills, particularly in Word, PowerPoint, Teams and Excel. · Ability to handle confidential information with discretion and professionalism. · Relevant 3 rd level HR qualification. **Desirable Criteria** * Experience with HR Locker or other similar HR system an advantage. * Garda Vetting and Safeguarding 1 training. **Key Qualities:** **· Excellent people skills** **· Understand how to build and align culture** **· Well developed conflict resolution and problem\-solving skills** **· Strong work ethic and ability to work in a dynamic environment** **· Ability to work under pressure** **· Flexible and adaptable attitude** **· Handling of sensitive information using discretion and confidentiality** **· Team player with ability to work on own initiative as well as in a small, dedicated leadership team.** **Additional Information** **● This is a two\-year fixed term contract, subject to a successful 6\-month period of probation.** **● The position will incorporate the need for flexible working hours including occasional evening and/or weekend work.** **● The position is full time based on a 37\.5 working hour week.** **● This position is based at our Head Office on the Sport Ireland Campus in Blanchardstown, Dublin.** **Remuneration:** Depending on experience. **Letter of application and CV should be sent by email to** hr@tennisireland.ie no later than **5:00pm on Friday the 27** **th** **of March 2026\.** Please reference ‘HR Generalist Role’ in the subject line. Late applications will not be accepted. All candidates must have existing valid permission to live and work in Ireland unrestricted. Appointment will be made subject to satisfactory Garda Vetting, Safeguarding and suitable reference checks. **Tennis Ireland is an equal opportunities employer.** -

title: Implementation Consultant (SaaS, €75k)
description: If you thrive on delivering complex, technical solutions, enjoy building things from scratch, and are ready to take ownership of post\-sale success for enterprise clients \- this is your next challenge. You're applying for a fast\-growing, globally trusted software (Series D, $100m ARR) company helping large organisations implement critical, highly customised solutions who are looking for a **Senior Implementation Consultant** to lead end\-to\-end delivery for strategic enterprise clients You'll be working directly with senior stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. It's a **hands\-on, high\-impact post\-sale delivery** , where your technical expertise, problem\-solving, and ability to thrive in ambiguity will drive success. **What You’ll Own:** * Lead **end\-to\-end post\-sale implementations** for enterprise clients. * Manage timelines, risks, and escalations while delivering **highly customised solutions** . * Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, Account Management, and Customer Success teams. * Translate complex customer requirements into **practical, technical implementations** . * Resolve complex technical and delivery issues, ensuring smooth, timely outcomes. * Contribute to and improve implementation methodologies and best practices. * Mentor and support junior implementation team members. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years implementing software for **enterprise or strategic customers** . * Proven track record delivering **complex, technical, post\-sale implementations** . * Direct customer\-facing experience with **large global organisations** (e.g., Booking.com, WorldPay). * Strong experience with **APIs (RESTful)** , JSON, JavaScript, SQL, and relational databases. * Comfortable managing multiple implementations in **ambiguous, first\-time delivery scenarios** . * Strong problem\-solving skills and **technical acumen** . * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Software, or related field preferred; formal PM certification is a plus but not essential. * Experience in SME or scale\-up environments preferred \- **mindset matters more than brand** . **The good stuff:** * **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – Tues/Wed/Thurs in office) * **Type:** Full\-time, permanent * **Salary:** Up to €75k \& benefits If you've read this far, it means there is clearly a level of interest and you should apply (If you are the right person of course!) If you want to **own enterprise post\-sale delivery** , work with complex systems, and make an impact from day one: APPLY NOW!

title: Infrastructure Credit – Core Portfolio Management - Associate -  Dublin
description: ****About This Role**** **Associate – Core Portfolio Management** **Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP), a part of BlackRock** ****About This Role:**** The Infra Credit platform spans all major infrastructure markets and sectors across the capital structure with $30\.7bn of assets under management across Investment grade and non\-investment grade strategies. The team has deployed nearly $29\.0 bn of capital across 270\+ assets. The GIP team are highly experienced with extensive expertise in originating, structuring and monitoring of Infrastructure investments which generate attractive risk adjusted returns with a focus on capital preservation and income enhancement. This is an exciting opportunity for a highly motivated and experienced professional to join the Core Portfolio Management Infrastructure Debt team supporting GIPs infrastructure credit business globally. The successful candidate will work closely with an experienced team in a collaborative and friendly environment. The role serves as a key liaison between internal teams, external partners and stakeholders, driving operational excellence and supporting the firm’s growth and performance objectives. This is a **hands‑on operational role** with exposure across the full investment lifecycle, from allocation assessment, deal onboarding and investment execution through to valuations, reporting, and ongoing fund and client support as well as opportunities to participate in fundraising processes. The Associate will play a key role in driving investment execution, maintaining strong controls and scalable processes as well as having opportunities to enhance the platforms data capabilities as GIP continues to grow and launch new products. ****Key Responsibilities:**** **Investment Process \& Valuations** * Co\-ordinate review and feedback on investment allocations and confirm adherence to Allocation Policy (e.g. investment guideline compliance, portfolio construction targets, etc.). * Complete pre\-trade compliance checks across all portfolios and mandates to confirm potential investment does not breach any agreed terms or investment guidelines * Anticipate and resolve operational issues arising during the evolving investment processes (e.g., trade execution, settlement, portfolio compliance, hedging, etc.) * Oversight of settlement day funding with our GATS/settlements team ensuring alignment with regards to settlement mechanics and investor nuances. * Work with external counterparties, namely, borrowers, advisors, sponsors to request specified documents; ensure they are provided in a timely and accurate manner * Integrate newly acquired portfolio assets ensuring accurate cusip set up and recording in Aladdin. * Assist in the co\-ordination of the monthly and quarterly valuation process, working with investment teams, valuation agent and pricing teams. * Assist in the review of monthly/quarterly valuation outputs to ensure reasonable questioning large variance month over month. * Develop and maintain relationships with key BlackRock/GIP operational professionals (e.g., legal, tax, trading operations, portfolio compliance, pricing, cash ops, bank loans, derivative operations and fund administration). * Deal management / reporting * Review monthly and quarterly client reporting ensuring accuracy and consistency and providing feedback to ACP team accordingly. * Ensure full and timely compliance with relevant regulatory reporting. * Assist in the provision of management information to senior management stakeholders * Enhance reporting quality and efficiency by improving Power BI dashboards and reducing manual workloads across the platform. * Assist in the preparation and presentation of board reports * Apply AI to streamline reporting and documentation i.e. for drafting of routine investor reports, extraction of key data from documentation to speed up processes etc. * Support responses to ad\-hoc client and internal data requests including investor specific reporting, liquidity analysis and operational queries. * Assist in reviewing operational implementation of side letters and client specific report obligations. * Contribute to the smooth operational launch of new strategies and accounts partnering closely with senior core pm team members. **Cash management** * Monitor liquidity for all private funds, including cash forecasting, borrowing capability, and dry powder. * Co\-ordination of investor capital calls and finance drawdowns to ensure funding requirements are met. * Co\-ordination of distributions from fund to investors. * Oversee the payables process where necessary. * Oversee the ongoing management of subscription lines and leverage facilities to ensure they are used optimally. * Partner with GATS, NAVO, asset management and investment team to ensure correct booking of interest payments, repayments of capital, and fee collection from underlying investments and through portfolio/fund structure **Process Improvement \& Controls** * Identify opportunities to improve, standardize and automate operational processes helping reduce manual effort and operational risk, considering AI to assist where appropriate. * Contribute to the documentation of procedures, controls and best practices as the platform continues to scale. * Participate in cross\-team initiatives aimed at strengthening Core PM’s operating model and integration within the broader BlackRock/GIP ecosystem. ****Development \& Growth Opportunities:**** **This role offers strong long\-term development potential within GIP, a part of BlackRock:** * End\-to\-end exposure to complex infrastructure investment structures, including leveraged and unlevered funds, SMAs, rated note structures and bespoke client solutions. * Hands‑on experience across portfolio operations, valuations, reporting, and client interaction, providing a deep understanding of the investment lifecycle. * Opportunities to contribute to process design, automation initiatives and platform build‑out, particularly as new funds and accounts launch. * Opportunity to join a high\-profile, fast\-growing and dynamic business that is a key strategic priority for the Firm. * Grow with a friendly entrepreneurial team at the center of the firm’s Private Debt business. * Significant exposure to globally oriented, prestige projects in a fast\-growing sector. * Strong brand in the Private Debt Industry ****Candidate Profile (indicative):**** * Ideally qualified or part qualified accountant. * Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, accounting, business management or * related discipline (or equivalent experience). * Strong attention to detail, organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment. * Self\-motivated with ability to operate with modest oversight. * Comfortable working across teams and with multiple stakeholders. * Proactive mindset with an interest in improving processes and building scalable solutions. * Strong financial modeling skills and proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including knowledge of advanced functions. * Interest in infrastructure investments and complex fund structures. * Experience in Python, SQL, Aladdin Software and Power BI will be viewed positively, but not required. ****Our Benefits**** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. ****About BlackRock**** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law.

title: Inside Sales Representative, India (based in Dublin)
description: Cohesity is the leader in AI\-powered data security. Over 13,600 enterprise customers, including over 85 of the Fortune 100 and nearly 70% of the Global 500, rely on Cohesity to strengthen their resilience while providing Gen AI insights into their vast amounts of data. Formed from the combination of Cohesity with Veritas’ enterprise data protection business, the company’s solutions secure and protect data on\-premises, in the cloud, and at the edge. Backed by NVIDIA, IBM, HPE, Cisco, AWS, Google Cloud, and others, Cohesity is headquartered in Santa Clara, CA, with offices around the globe. We’ve been named a Leader by multiple analyst firms and have been globally recognized for Innovation, Product Strength, and Simplicity in Design , and our culture. **Want to join the leader in AI\-powered data security?** The **Inside Sales Representative** is responsible for driving revenue through proactive sales activities, lead nurturing, and deal closure. The ideal candidate is a self\-motivated professional with strong communication skills, a passion for sales, and a demonstrated track record of consistently meeting or exceeding targets. **HOW YOU'LL SPEND YOUR TIME HERE:** * Generate and qualify new sales opportunities by engaging inbound leads and executing targeted outbound outreach (phone, email, meetings). * Conduct discovery conversations to uncover prospect needs, pain points, business drivers, solution fit, and decision\-making timelines. * Research target accounts, identify key decision\-makers, and develop tailored engagement strategies using tools such as HG Insights, Marketo, 6sense, ZoomInfo, Outreach, and LinkedIn. * Identify and build relationships with key partners to extend reach, expand coverage, and accelerate pipeline creation; train and role play with partners on how to build pipeline by positioning Cohesity's SaaS value aligned with GTM initiatives. * Partner closely with Account Executives, Sales Engineers, and Sales Leadership to execute the go\-to\-market strategy for the assigned territory, to build and implement territory plans, prioritize accounts, shape deals, and advance qualified opportunities. * Manage a territory, owning SMB or commercial accounts and selling specific product sets: build and maintain a rolling 3\-month pipeline equal to 6X quarterly quota, achieve/exceed quarterly goals, close deals, and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities. * Deliver effective product demos that showcase the value of Cohesity's SaaS solutions; maintain deep knowledge of offerings, product capabilities, industry trends, and top competitors to differentiate effectively. * Analyze outreach and qualification performance, identify optimization opportunities, and collaborate with leadership on updated strategies, messaging, and processes. * Mentor and support SDRs and Sales Associates through best\-practice sharing, coaching, and onboarding support. **WE'D LOVE TO TALK TO YOU IF YOU HAVE MANY OF THE FOLLOWING:** * Must be based in Dublin, Ireland and legally eligible to work full time longterm in Ireland. * Passion for sales, with at least 3 years of relevant experience preferred. * Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets * Proficient in CRM systems and prospecting tools. * Skilled in researching companies, conducting cold calls and email campaigns, navigating organizational charts, and using creative strategies to reach prospects. * Ability to work independently, stay organized, and manage multiple priorities effectively. * Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills. * Coachable, with a strong desire to learn and apply feedback. * Demonstrates resilience and grit, quickly recovering from setbacks. * Exceptional work ethic, consistently striving to exceed targets and outperform peers. * Collaborative team player, eager to share best practices and support colleagues. * Self\-motivated and driven, committed to ongoing professional growth. * College or bachelor’s degree. * Vocational training, completion of the Cohesity Sales Associate program, or a minimum of 3 years of relevant experience preferred. * Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and support decision making **Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates: For information on personal data processing, please see our* Privacy Policy *.** **Equal Employment Opportunity Employer (EEOE) Cohesity is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status or any other category protected by law. If you are an individual with a disability and require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are limited in the ability or unable to access or use this online application process and need an alternative method for applying, you may contact us at 1\-855\-9COHESITY or* talent@cohesity.com *for assistance.** **In\-Office Expectations Cohesity employees who are within a reasonable commute (e.g. within a forty\-five (45\) minute average travel time) work out of our core offices 2\-3 days a week of their choosing.**

title: Intern Revenue Enablement Specialist (d/f/m)
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **This role requires 3 days in the office and is based in Dublin.** Looking for an internship or a working student job? Work directly with our teams and gain practical, hands\-on experience in an environment that is both challenging and fun \- ideal for those eager to learn, develop, and take every opportunity for growth. At Personio, we’re looking for a proactive, tech\-savvy intern to support our Revenue Enablement team. You will play a key role in driving enablement initiatives that empower our customer\-facing teams through efficient data, content, and learning management. This is a great opportunity for students or recent graduates who thrive in dynamic environments and are eager to launch a career in revenue enablement, learning and development, or business operations. **What You'll Do** * Data Management: Update and maintain master enablement records, learning records, calendars, mailing lists, and tool user groups; proactively collect attendance and feedback inputs. * Learning Experience Design: Populate templates with enablement content and support the creation of learning assets for business\-critical initiatives (e.g., onboarding, rebrand). * Knowledge Management: Migrate and maintain internal content in Confluence using templates and governance; support external content migrations (e.g., Highspot → Trumpet) and keep baseline materials current. * Field Effectiveness: Prepare FAQs and documentation following enablement sessions or user testing, and provide ad hoc project support as needed. * Coordination: Manage enablement program calendars, distribute training schedules, own invites, and support the onboarding team with day\-to\-day coordination and administrative tasks. **What You Need To Succeed** * Enrollment \& Availability: Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Business, Commerce, Communications, Project Management, or a related field and available to work 20 hours per week. * Interest \& Motivation: Keen interest in adult learning, technology, and international business. * Attention to Detail: Organized, diligent, and detail\-oriented—able to manage multiple tasks with a high bar for quality. * Tech Savvy: Quick to learn new software and tools; comfortable working across enablement and knowledge platforms. * Tools \& Collaboration: Skilled in Google Sheets and digital collaboration platforms (e.g., Confluence/Atlassian tools). * Automation \& AI Mindset: Interested in automation and AI\-driven solutions, always looking for smarter ways of working. * Communication \& Teamwork: Proactive team player with strong communication skills and a collaborative, can\-do attitude. **Why Personio:** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race. At Personio, we value in\-person collaboration while also offering flexibility. This role is office\-based, with certain days every week required in your contracted office location. The remaining days can be worked from home or in the office if you prefer. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work: * Choose a subsidy for public transportation or a gym membership * No matter where you are in your life right now –after a gap year or during your studies – we offer both mandatory and voluntary internship opportunities * Enjoy a buzzy, open office spaces with coffee, drinks, snacks, and fresh fruit in every central location But we even go beyond of that \- we want to guarantee your continuous growth: * Continuous feedback and acknowledgement with your manager in weekly 1:1s * Build connections through our strong internal student network and regular meet\-ups with colleagues from different departments * Join our in\-house knowledge\-sharing sessions, internal buddy program, and work productively with the latest hardware and tools Salary: * Interns: 20 hours per week; 1\.232€ per month Get a sneak peek of your potential upcoming workplace on our Instagram or TikTok @inside.personio

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92004 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Program, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise—while making real contributions to Autodesk’s design, manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns—locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally—through programs designed just for interns. You’ll work with a team of Developer Advocates experts in a dynamic, supportive environment, collaborating on the definition and development of core technologies. Along the way, you’ll gain exposure to our exciting tech stack, which includes modelling, Design Data, AI \& DevOps tools. You will be working closely with the engineering team developing the core software building blocks on which current and future Autodesk products are being built. We are looking for someone who is enthusiastic about learning new technologies and to promote technologies to Autodesk partners. You are able to face new technologies and challenges and work in a dynamic and collaborative environment with some very passionate and awesome people. If you’re an innovative and fun team player, who strives to learn and improve, you’ll fit right in. We are a global team, and you will collaborate with a variety of people and teams from around the world. Take an active role in writing code samples, work on proof of concept using rapid prototyping principles and present technology in various events. Produce high quality readable code, covered with tests, and participate in code reviews. Continuously learn and improve your coding and design skills. **Several potential projects are available,** and we’ll assign you to the one that best aligns with your **interests and skills** , ensuring the role is tailored to you while giving you meaningful, impactful work. **Responsibilities** * Prototype, implement, and maintain production software components and cloud services. * Collaborate with experienced engineers to design, plan, develop, refactor, test, deliver, and maintain complex features across desktop, cloud and web components. * Participate actively in Agile development using LEAN methodology as a member of a scrum team. * Gain hands\-on experience with commercial development processes used at Autodesk. * Collaborate with engineering mentors to define project scope and milestones. * Present progress and final outcomes to internal stakeholders. **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as an active student in a degree program. * Predicted First or Upper Second\-Class degree in STEM Field (e.g., Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Physics) * Please include your estimated graduation year and month in your application. * Familiarity with AI/ML concepts, APIs, and agent frameworks. * Experience with databases, REST/JSON\-RPC APIs, or web development is a plus. * Passion for developer tools, open\-source, and AI innovation. **The Ideal Candidate** * Interest in design, 3D modelling or geometry, and technology. * Experience in any programming language such as .NET, and/or Typescript. * Experience in Python for AI. Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Investment Associate
description: Cairn has a unique opportunity for a high calibre Investment Associate to join our team. **Responsibilities:** * Support underwriting of acquisitions and existing development opportunities including financial modelling, investment memos, deal structuring and due diligence. * Providing accurate financial modelling and scenario planning to enable effective decision making across the portfolio. * Work cross functionally in compiling, preparing and finalising development appraisals and modelling to support key decision making. * Support the Investment Team in gateway review stages, ensuring that financial, commercial, and design teams are aligned before presentation to senior leadership team members. * Support the long‑term land pipeline planning through site‑by‑site optimisation \& exit strategy * Assist in preparing weekly functional presentations for senior leadership team. * Conduct market research including researching market trends, competitor positioning, and policy changes influencing acquisition strategy and broader portfolio positioning. **Requirements:** * Degree or relevant experience in real estate, finance, economics etc. * Strong financial modelling capability. * Keen interest in development. * 3\-5 years’ experience. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * Strong presentation skills. * Strong problem solving and attention to detail\-orientation. * Highly commercial, proactive and comfortable working in an entrepreneurial environment. **Working with Cairn Homes** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join Cairn which include * Competitive salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Full health insurance with Irish Life health * Annual salary review * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Free Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * Employee Assistance Programme with VHI * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture Committee * Talent Development Programmes * Approved Profit Sharing Scheme (APSS) * Company vehicle (role dependent) The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.

title: Investment Associate
description: We are currently partnering with a leading global fund manager with an established credit investment platform based in Dublin. **Key Responsibilities:** * Lead fundamental credit analysis across leveraged loans, high\-yield bonds, and direct lending opportunities. * Build, review, and take ownership of detailed financial models to support investment decisions. * Prepare high\-quality investment papers and present recommendations to the Investment Committee. * Conduct comprehensive due diligence on issuers, including in\-depth assessment of management teams and business models. * Actively monitor portfolio positions, proactively identifying risks, catalysts, and value creation opportunities. * Contribute to investment strategy discussions, idea generation, and relative value analysis. * Mentor and support junior team members where appropriate. **Key Requirements:** * 3–5 years’ relevant experience in credit investing, leveraged finance, investment banking, or corporate finance. * Strong academic track record; ACA / CFA or equivalent highly desirable. * Advanced financial modelling and Excel skills, with strong analytical and technical capability. * Solid understanding of capital structures, credit documentation, and debt instruments. * Proven ability to form independent investment views and articulate them clearly. * Strong communication skills with the confidence to operate effectively in a front\-office environment. * Demonstrated interest in financial markets, with a proactive, driven, and commercially focused mindset. *Due to high volume of applications received only qualified candidates will be contacted in relation to position.*

title: Junior Consultant - Transition From Any Background
description: 1. **Control Your Earnings: Uncapped commission – the more you deliver, the more you take home.** 2. **Progress When You Perform: No waiting years. Hit targets, get promoted, repeat.** 3. **Win With a Team That Wins: Ambitious peers, ideas that get heard, and wins that get recognised.** Join **Access** as we expand across Ireland and Northern Ireland. If you're commercially minded and want direct control over how fast your career and earnings grow, this is where performance truly pays. This role isn’t for everyone – but if you’re competitive, driven to succeed, and motivated by progression and high earnings, keep reading. **What You’ll Gain in This Role** 1. **Merit\-Based Promotions:** Progression is entirely performance\-driven – results move you forward. 2. **Accelerated Career Growth:** Many team members achieve multiple promotions within their first two years, with some moving up in as little as 6 months. 3. **Direct Access to Leadership:** Flat structure with open communication and mentors invested in your success. 4. **A Voice That Actually Matters:** Ideas are welcomed, collaboration is encouraged, and your impact is recognised. **What We’re Looking For** * Minimum 6 months post\-graduation experience * Ambition to build a career in a fast\-paced, commercial environment * Clear, confident communicator who enjoys building relationships * Availability for a fully office\-based role in Dublin 2 (Monday–Friday) * EU Passport or Stamp 4 Visa required **The Role** * Source and connect with high\-quality candidates across multiple industries * Build relationships with professionals looking to advance their careers * Manage the recruitment process from first conversation to successful placement * Work closely with clients to understand hiring needs and deliver talent solutions * Drive revenue through successful placements and business development activity For more information, contact Nadia Garcia on \+353 1 572 1159 or email your CV to nadia.garcia@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: Junior Consultant – Full training provided
description: * **Expert training from day one \- start strong, learn fast, and crush it from the jump.** * **Constant growth \- never settle, always level up, and keep your skills unstoppable.** * **Certified \& credible \- earn your stripes, stand out, and own your field.** Are you stuck in customer service or sales, hitting targets but seeing no real growth? Ready for a career where your results actually fuel your success. At **Access Healthcare** , we specialize in helping driven professionals from all kinds of backgrounds launch successful careers in recruitment. Our team is growing, and we’re looking for ambitious people ready to thrive in a fast\-paced, results\-driven environment and become Recruitment Consultants with us. **Why choose Access Healthcare for your career move?** * **From Zero to Expert:** 200\+ training modules, 1:1 coaching, and continuous development, you’ll never feel stuck. * **Continuous Development:** You’ll never feel stagnant. With nonstop training, you’ll keep developing your skills and growing your career. * **Get Certified:** Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant and build credibility that opens doors. **What You Will Need** * At least 2 year of experience in a sales\-focused role (e.g., Sales Rep, Sales Associate, Account Executive, Business Development, Inside sales, Tele sales representative, Sales Manager, Customer Success Manager, etc.) * An ambitious, growth\-driven mindset * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent is a plus but not required. **Benefits of being part of our team:** * Average first\-year total compensation **€43K\+** , second year **€61K\+** , with high performers exceeding this. * **Uncapped commission structure.** * **Mentorship from industry leaders.** * **Leadership Academy \& ongoing training.** * **Supportive team culture** with wellness programs, social committee, and flexi\-benefits. Ready for a new, exciting opportunity? Contact Maria de Gouveia at maria.degouveia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1202 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity. Take your career to new heights!

title: Junior Contract Administrator
description: **Company Description** Turner \& Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. We are majority\-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner \& Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world. Please visit our website: www.turnerandtownsend.com **Job Description** The Junior Contract Administrator is responsible for providing support to the Commercial Administration Lead regarding the management and administration of contracts within the client organisation. The role holder is responsible for the day\-to\-day administrative tasks related to contract management, including drafting, reviewing and managing contracts. The Junior Contract Administrator ensures all contracts comply with legal and organisational guidelines. The role holder prepares and organises contract related documents, maintains record and supports contract negotiations. **Principal Accountabilities** * Assist in drafting, reviewing, and finalising of all contracts, ensuring that all terms and conditions are clear and comply with the client organisations polices and legal requirements. * Maintain organised and up\-to\-date records of all contracts and related documents, ensuring that all contract documentation is stored securely and is easily accessible. * Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with contract administration. * Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders regarding contract\-related inquiries. * Update and maintain the contract management system with accurate and current information. * Communicate contract terms and conditions to internal and external parties. * Track contract milestones, deadlines, and renewals. * Generate reports on contract status and performance, providing support during contract negotiations. * Manage contract\-related data, including contract management software or databases. * Assist with invoicing, payment processing, and other financial tasks related to contracts. * Prepare reports on contract status, performance, and compliance. * Support communication and collaboration with vendors and suppliers. * Perform additional tasks, activities or other duties as may be required or assigned as part of the role. **Experience** * 3 years’ experience in a contract administration support role in a high value rail projects or projects of a similar scale / complexity. * Experience in assisting with the drafting, reviewing, and administration of commercial contracts. * Experience dealing with a range of stakeholders at all levels across the organisation. **Skills** * Good influencing skills to manage and motivate teams in support of delivery of high risk, value and complex project and programmes. * Ability to coordinate and organise multiple sources of reporting inputs / performance data and deliver project / programme reports as required. * An ability to work effectively in a team environment. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills to facilitate clear and consistent dialogue between all parties involved. * Ability to govern and drive outcomes to ensure high quality service. * Comprehensive knowledge of cost management principles, methods, techniques and tools, including the standard method of measurement. **Qualifications** * C Eng / BEng / BA / BSc cost management, quantity surveying, estimating management or in a related discipline. **Additional Information** **What we offer you:** * Full time * Competitive remuneration and attractive range of benefits * 8% Pension * 23 days Annual leave (\+ 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and collaborative colleagues Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance. Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community. Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn

management principles, methods, techniques and tools, including the standard method of measurement. **Qualifications** * C Eng / BEng / BA / BSc cost management, quantity surveying, estimating management or in a related discipline. **Additional Information** **What we offer you:** * Full time * Competitive remuneration and attractive range of benefits * 8% Pension * 23 days Annual leave (\+ 2 company days) * Opportunity to work on impactful and innovative projects * Career development opportunities both in Ireland and globally * Opportunity to work with a diverse group of talented and collaborative colleagues Our inspired people share our vision and mission.
