title: Junior Sales Specialist
description: **AuxiliaGroup Recruitment are currently hiring a Junior Sales Specialist on behalf of our client, an established company within the healthcare sector. This is a permanent opportunity based in Dublin.** **Role:** * Gathering market and customer information to figure out the customer needs * Responding to customer queries and resolving their objections * Advising product developers on improvements to include in forthcoming product developments and discussing special promotions * Inspecting inventory in stock and the quality of the product on display **Requirements:** * 1\+ years sales related experience required. * Flexibility to travel to and work with clients when needed. * Target driven individual * Excellent communication skills, ability to develop and grow client relationships Full licence required for this position.

title: Junior Site Engineer
description: **Junior Site Engineer – Civil Infrastructure** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a **Graduate / Junior Site Engineer** to join their growing team across projects in **Meath** and surrounding areas. This is an excellent opportunity for a final\-year student or recent graduate to gain hands\-on experience on large\-scale civil infrastructure and public realm projects. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the delivery of civil infrastructure projects including roads, cycle lanes, drainage, and public realm upgrades * Support site setting out, surveying, and quality control * Monitor project progress and report to senior engineers and project managers * Coordinate with subcontractors and site teams * Ensure compliance with health \& safety standards and company procedures * Assist in preparing site reports and documentation **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering (or related discipline) – graduating May/June or recently qualified * **Must have completed some form of work placement or internship during studies (even short\-term), ideally within civil engineering, construction, or utilities** * Strong communication and organisational skills * Willingness to work on\-site across multiple locations * Basic knowledge of AutoCAD or Civil 3D is an advantage * Full driving licence preferred **What’s on Offer** * **€40k–€45k starting salary** * Exposure to major civil and public realm projects * Structured mentoring and career development * Clear progression pathway within a leading contractor **To apply:** If you are a motivated Graduate / Junior Engineer looking to kick\-start your career, please send your CV or contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com *GPC will not send your CV to clients without prior discussion and consent.*

title: Junior To Mid-Level EHS Professional
description: **Company Description** Sustainable Safety Ltd specializes in providing Health and Safety consultancy and training services across Ireland, with a focus on promoting workplace safety and compliance. The company offers a wide range of solutions, including Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), workwear, and services related to Working at Height (WAH) and lifting equipment. Our mission is to support businesses in achieving their safety and environmental goals. We pride ourselves on delivering expert knowledge, tailored guidance, and practical solutions for improving workplace health and safety standards. **Role Description** This is a full\-time, on\-site role located in Grange Castle, Dublin for a Junior to Mid\-Level EHS Professional. The role involves supporting the implementation and management of Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS) systems and processes. Core responsibilities include conducting risk assessments, developing and delivering training programs, monitoring compliance, ensuring the safe use of equipment like PPE and lifting tools, and promoting a culture of workplace safety and environmental awareness. Collaboration with teams to improve occupational health practices and compliance will be key. **Qualifications** * Expertise in Environment Health \& Safety (EHS) and Health \& Safety practices * Strong knowledge of Environmental Awareness and Occupational Health policies * Strong problem\-solving and organizational skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for working with diverse teams * Relevant certifications or qualifications in Health and Safety, EHS, or related fields * Familiarity with Irish health and safety regulations is advantageous * Experience working with PPE and WAH or lifting equipment is considered a plus

title: Leveraged Finance Analyst/Associate
description: We are currently partnering with a leading global fund manager with an established credit investment platform based in Dublin. **Key Responsibilities:** * Conduct in\-depth fundamental credit analysis across leveraged loans, high\-yield bonds, and direct lending opportunities. * Build and maintain complex financial models (LBO, cash flow, and capital structure analysis) to support investment decisions. * Evaluate new investment opportunities and develop clear, well\-supported investment theses. * Prepare and present investment memoranda to the Investment Committee. * Perform detailed due diligence, including financial, commercial, and management assessment. * Monitor and manage existing portfolio positions, identifying risks, catalysts, and value creation opportunities. * Contribute to idea generation, relative value analysis, and broader portfolio construction discussions. **Key Requirements:** * 2–4 years’ experience in leveraged finance, investment banking, private credit, or credit investing. * Strong academic background; ACA, CFA, or equivalent qualification preferred. * Advanced financial modelling and Excel skills, with strong analytical and technical capabilities. * Solid understanding of capital structures, credit documentation, and debt instruments. * Demonstrated ability to form independent investment views and articulate them clearly. * Strong communication skills and confidence operating in a front\-office, investment\-driven environment. * High level of motivation, commercial acumen, and a genuine interest in leveraged finance and credit markets. *Due to high volume of applications received only qualified candidates will be contacted in relation to position.*

title: Marketing Assistant
description: Ireland’s leading full\-service law firm, Byrne Wallace Shields LLP, is seeking a Marketing Assistant Graduate to join the firm’s dynamic and fast paced Marketing Team. This position reports directly to the Head of Marketing and is a generalist role with an emphasis on supporting events, internal communications, and creating content and digital marketing campaigns, including associated graphics and multimedia. This is an excellent opportunity to work in a rapidly growing firm, where you will work directly with senior partners and specialist legal teams, helping to drive multi\-channel marketing campaigns whilst providing proactive and high\-quality marketing support, and supporting the delivery of excellent client service further strengthening of the firm’s position as a leading business law firm. The working environment is both challenging and interesting as we are at the cutting edge of the market across many of our legal areas. **Key Responsibilities** * Events: supporting with the firm’s Events Programme, including event management and communications, booking external events and maintaining a calendar of relevant external events. * PR, Advertising \& Sponsorships: supporting press announcements and photoshoots, drafting press releases, researching media opportunities, implementing third party partnerships and sponsorship/speaking arrangements. * Directory Listings: supporting the firm’s annual directory submission process, and any third\-party directory profiling opportunities. * Brand \& Design: coordinating and designing marketing collateral and graphics (including PowerPoint presentations, posters, invitations, brochures, digital graphics), liaising with external suppliers where relevant, and supporting any brand and messaging projects as they arise. * CRM: managing CRM campaigns and using the firm's CRM database to input and maintain information on clients and contacts, including mailing list management, and tracking marketing related CRM activities. * Web \& Digital: supporting management of the firm’s website, social media channels, email marketing campaigns, video and audio creation. * Content Marketing: supporting initiatives to create content and thought leadership, and optimising distribution across multiple digital channels. * Supporting internal communications and internal marketing * Market Research \& Data Analysis: monitoring and tracking the firm’s online and social presence, digital and off\-line marketing campaigns, CRM activities, and other general market and competitor analysis. * Support with general marketing administration **Skills and Experience** * Third level qualification in marketing or equivalent. * Experience working in a B2B or professional services firm. * Strong Microsoft PowerPoint, Word and Excel skills. * End\-to\-end event management experience is advantageous * Proficient in web and social media management, email marketing, webinars, and video/audio creation. Experience using Vutur, Wordpress, InterAction (CRM), and Teams is advantageous * Experience of graphic design packages e.g. Canva, InDesign, Photoshop. * Experience in and understanding of Client Relationship Management is advantageous * Understanding of content marketing and proven experience drafting content and optimising distribution. * Experience undertaking marketing research and data analysis. **Personal Attributes** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, in particular good copywriting and proof\-reading skills. * Strong multi\-tasker, well organised with the ability to work to short deadlines. * A solid understanding of web based and digital marketing best practice including Google analytics, and SEO. * A good understanding of AI tools and their role and function within a marketing team. * Excellent customer service skills. * Creative, with a good eye for design. * Team player. * Energetic, proactive and ability to work on own initiative. * Confident and personable.

title: Marketing Associate
description: Marketing Associate Role Dublin A key client of mine is looking for a Marketing Associate to join their team. This person will be a key contributor to the development and execution of marketing strategies, supporting product growth while delivering value to customers and end users. **Key Responsibilities** * Support product branding, communications, and promotional activities to deliver on brand strategy * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams, including sales and other internal stakeholders, to ensure aligned execution * Plan and deliver marketing projects with guidance from senior team members * Provide marketing support across a portfolio of products, ensuring consistent implementation of key initiatives * Monitor and manage budgets, identifying and addressing any variances against plan * Coordinate and organise cross\-functional team meetings to support effective decision\-making * Support the planning and execution of events and industry conferences * Manage logistics for internal and external meetings, ensuring compliance with relevant policies and regulations * Deliver multichannel marketing activities, including digital and print campaigns, to enhance engagement * Oversee the management of promotional materials, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and availability * Work with internal teams and external agencies to develop high\-quality marketing content * Ensure materials are reviewed and approved through internal compliance processes in a timely manner **Skills \& Experience** * Degree\-level education or equivalent * Experience managing multiple projects in a fast\-paced, collaborative environment * Experience supporting digital marketing initiatives across multiple channels * Understanding of working within a regulated environment * Strong analytical, numerical, and problem\-solving skills * Ability to interpret data and technical or scientific information * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and familiarity with digital or AI\-enabled tools **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to** **jburke@berkley\-group.com** **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to www.berkley\-group.com.**

title: Mechanical Estimator
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe's only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. We are pleased to announce that we are currently seeking a **Mechanical Estimator** due to our continued success within the Data Centre Industry. The position will be based in our Head Office in Ballymount, Dublin. **Position:** Mechanical Estimator **Location:** Ballymount, Dublin 22 **Reporting to:** Estimating Director **Responsibilities** * Produce reports for management for bids and monthly meetings * Prepare estimates based on drawings and specifications * Manage quantities and prepare pricing document * Oversea pricing for elements of the building from the Irish and European subcontractors and suppliers * Utilise spreadsheets to compare subcontractors bids and quotations, and to demonstrate reasoning for selecting subcontractors * Assist in the maintenance of the cost data base * Present the estimate to the Estimating Director, and to the Board of Directors, where and when required * Build relationships with key vendors (subcontractors, suppliers, engineers etc) * Keep informed with developments in the construction industry * Participate in the development of procedures to ensure a high standard of quality control in the documents produced by the Estimating Department * Co\-operate with the application of quality assurance procedures * Mentor and train junior members of the team * Co\-ordinate and collaborate with the CSA \& Electrical Estimators **Requirements** * 4\+ years experience within a Mechanical capacity * Estimating or Quantity Surveying background * Knowledge of FIDIC contracts is desirable * Proficiency in the use of Microsoft Word, Excel, and in at least one of the more widely used QS BQ production software packages \- e.g. Buildsoft or CostX * Understand the requirement to achieve a high level of quality in the production of subcontractor tender documentation and Winthrop's tender submission * Be able to identify potential risks on a project * Be aware of the company's high standards of safety **Benefits** * Opportunity to work with a Market Leader in the Data centre Industry * Supportive career development * Competitive salary package * Collaborative team environment * Pipeline of project growth ****Winthrop Technologies Ltd are an equal opportunities employer****

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: Assets and Operations Europe Region DUB0000S1 **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting team supports clients across the full asset lifecycle—from acquisition and planning through to operation and disposal—by addressing the interdependencies between people, processes, systems, and assets. The discipline delivers a broad and integrated set of services, including: * **Operational Strategy \& Transformation:** Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * **Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC):** Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * **Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement:** Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * **Digital Enablement:** Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for students who are currently studying for a relevant bachelor’s degree level or be currently undertaking a post\-graduate level qualification in a relevant discipline, including, but not limited to: * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity We also value: * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern \#LI\-Onsite **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Operations and Logistics Associate
description: **🌍 About CRYOPDP** **CRYOPDP** provides reliable, temperature\-controlled transport solutions tailored to the Life Sciences and Healthcare industries, supporting the demands of global healthcare logistics. With operations in 15 countries across the Americas, EMEA, and APAC, CRYOPDP combines local expertise with global reach, delivering customized logistics solutions that meet the highest standards of quality, compliance, and reliability. As part of **DHL Supply Chain** , integrating the Pharma Specialized network, CRYOPDP leverages its established expertise in temperature\-controlled logistics tailored for the pharmaceutical, biopharmaceutical, and advanced therapies communities, ensuring that critical shipments reach patients safely and on time anywhere in the world. **Our mission is clear** : Support the health of people around the world by providing global innovative temperature\-controlled logistics solutions to the Clinical Research and Cell \& Gene Therapy Communities. CRYOPDP. Logistics with a purpose. Designed to save lives. **🎯 Overall Mission** The mission of our Operations and Logistics Associate is to act in a cross functional and versatile capacity to ensure the smooth handling of project documentation and communications associated with order processing, inventory management, import and export processes, last mile and white glove delivery projects. This requires splitting time between warehouse, logistics and office operations. The ideal candidate is organized, detail\-oriented, and comfortable working in a dynamic operational environment, and engaging in all activities, connected with our Dublin office. Initially, this role will include: * Day\-to\-day management of Medidis project documentation (explanatory training provided) * Preparation of packaging materials * Assisting with product returns * Order preparation * General warehouse duties, deliveries, airport collections * Supporting pick\-and\-pack projects * Carrying out additional ad hoc tasks as required The role also involves working closely with logistics service providers (LSPs), customers, and intercompany partners, in addition to generating monthly reports on various KPI’s and and customer performance indicators. **🔑 Main Contributions** *Medidis Project Admin* * Assist with project documentation (returns sheets \& TIC sheets) * Assist with management of database * Assist with management of spreadsheets * Order processing * Manifest generation * Shipper tracking *Warehouse* * Assisting with receipt and processing of returns * Inspections and sanitization * Pre\-conditioning and conditioning of shippers (per work instructions) * Preparation of orders * Outer packaging assembly * Assistance with general warehouse duties as and when required *White Glove Delivery Service* * Preparation of daily manifest for shipments * Direct liaison with customers for booking of shipments * Engaging with operations team internally on route planning and schedules *Last Mile Delivery Service* * Direct liaison with customers on identification of shipper requirement * Processing internal orders for shipper use * Preparation of shippers for last mile project * Engagement with national courier to generate bookings as applicable *Import / Export Services* * Booking of flights for cargo * Compiling associated documentation * Engaging with customs teams on customs clearance * Collections from Airport as required * Processing on TMS *Ad hoc duties* * Assisting team with general administration * Assisting with welcoming visitors * Providing cover for team mates annual leave * Providing reasonable assistance as and where required **🎓 Requirements** * Warehouse/ Distribution experience of min 1 year * Experience in a similar industry * Fluency in English **🧠 Specific Knowledge** * Full clean drivers license is essential * IT Literacy **⭐ Essential Requirements** * Versatile, with a “can do” attitude * Excellent communication skills * Sharp attention to detail * Flexible, reliable \& loyal team player * Motivated, hard\-working and dependable; Time keeping \- attendance and punctuality are of the utmost importance due to the nature of the business **🌟 Why Join CRYOPDP?** * Make a tangible impact on patients’ lives worldwide * Join a global, purpose\-driven organization * Work in a specialized, regulated industry * Opportunities for career development and growth

title: Peer Reviewer
description: On behalf of our Health Research client in Dublin, we are hiring 2 Annual Peer Reviewers on a 16 week contract. The two technical experts are required to review approximately 226 Annual Reports\- * Assessing scientific progress against agreed objectives * Highlighting, where appropriate, any areas that require further clarification in terms of: * scientific progress * research personnel * timelines * research governance (e.g. ethics approval, animal licenses) and * attainment of agreed deliverables and/or milestones * expenditure and adherence to budget * Any other issues as relevant * Presenting summaries of the detailed reviews carried out in the form of a weekly progress meeting. Reviewers are responsible for ensuring that any issues with the grants are captured and communicated accurately at the weekly meetings and resolved in a timely manner. **Qualifications:** The technical experts should have a primary degree and post\-graduate degree ( **PhD, OR a Master’s degree with at least three year’s appropriate research / professional experience** ) in a health\-related discipline, preferably in patient oriented, health services or population health research. The technical experts should possess a high degree of computer literacy, including good working knowledge and experience of using Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook and experience of working with databases. Essential competencies include: * A commitment to quality and strong attention to detail * Excellent analytical skills * Excellent communications skills * Excellent planning and organising abilities * A high degree of motivation and professional integrity Get in touch today!

title: Plant Area Assistant
company: The Garden Café at Johnstown Garden Centre
description: **Join Johnstown Garden Centre \- A Tradition in Quality Since 1974** Johnstown Garden Centre, one of Ireland’s leading garden retailers, is looking for a Full\-Time Plant Area Assistant to support our team with general operational duties in the Plant Area. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist in maintaining the plant area by ensuring it is clean, well\-organised, and fully stocked. * Support with receiving and unloading plant deliveries, ensuring stock is stored correctly. * Assist customers in the plant area, providing basic help with their enquiries. * Help manage trolleys, ensuring they are available and easy for customers to use. * Keep the plant displays neat and ensure all signage and plant labelling is up to date. * Regularly sweep and tidy the plant area to maintain a clean and welcoming environment. * Work alongside the team to support general operations and ensure the smooth running of the plant area. * Follow all health and safety procedures, maintaining a safe working environment. **The Ideal Candidate:** * Physically fit and able to perform manual tasks, such as unloading deliveries and moving plants. * Comfortable working outdoors and in a garden environment. * Friendly, approachable, and willing to assist customers where needed. * Team\-oriented, with the ability to work efficiently in a busy environment. * Flexible, adaptable, and proactive in assisting with day\-to\-day tasks. * A positive attitude with a strong work ethic. **Experience and Qualifications:** * No experience in horticulture or retail is necessary, though it would be an advantage. * Candidates with a background in general labour or similar roles are welcome. * A passion for gardening and the willingness to learn more about plants is beneficial. **Hours of Work:** * Full\-time position with flexibility to work 5 out of 7 days, including weekends and bank holidays. * Rotating roster system to cover our opening hours: Monday to Saturday 9:00am to 6:00pm, Sunday \& Bank Holidays 10:00am to 6:00pm. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €14\.15 per hour Expected hours: No less than 40 per week Work Location: In person

title: Project Engineer
description: **BnM is currently looking for a motivated and experienced Project Engineer to join our Renewables team in Newbridge. The Project Engineer with the PMO supports the delivery of BnM’s large\-scale renewable energy infrastructure projects across all phases, from development through to operation. Working under the guidance of Senior Project Managers and the PMO, the role ensures project activities progress according to schedule, budget, and governance standards, contributing to the company’s low\-carbon and renewable energy goals.** The Role: The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: * Assist Senior Project Managers in planning, coordinating, and monitoring project activities across all phases. Maintain project schedules, action logs, and milestone tracking within the PMO framework. Scope, plan and co\-ordinate activities on assigned work packages to successfully deliver required outputs. Ensure tasks assigned to functional teams are progressing as planned. * Support the preparation, review, and administration of contracts, purchase orders, and procurement documents. Help track project budgets, expenditures, and financial reporting under the oversight of Senior Project Managers. Contribute to maintaining accurate project documentation and financial records. * Support the team in tracking planning consents, permits, and regulatory approvals required for construction and operation. Ensure documentation is maintained to demonstrate compliance with regulatory and environmental requirements. * Assist in maintaining effective communication with internal teams, advisors, suppliers, and external stakeholders. Help prepare reports, presentations, and updates for PMO, Senior Project Managers, and Program Managers. Participate in project meetings and follow up on assigned actions. * Support risk identification, monitoring, and reporting under the guidance of Senior Project Managers. Assist in preparing operational handover documentation to support readiness for project commissioning. * Maintain accurate project records and dashboards to support PMO reporting. Contribute to project governance by following PMO methodologies and processes. Capture lessons learned and share observations to improve project delivery practices. * All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises * Proactively participate in the Performance Management process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's, objectives. The Person: The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: * A third\-level degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline (Master’s degree advantageous). * Minimum 3 years’ experience in large scale energy or infrastructure project delivery * Competency across a broad range project management skills must be demonstrated. Familiarity with energy infrastructure sector in Ireland. * Excellent technical appraisal, report writing and communications skills essential. * Professional project management certification (e.g. PMP or equivalent) is desirable. * Proficient in MS Office and MS Project. * Post graduate qualification in project management is desirable If you wish to be considered, simply apply on or before **10th April 2026** . **BnM** **is an equal opportunities employer.**

title: Project Engineer
description: Established in 1998, Celtic Anglian Water (CAW) is a subsidiary company of the UK’s *Anglian Water Group.* Headquartered in Dublin, CAW employs over 90 people at various offices and treatment plants in Ireland and the UK. Regarded as one of Ireland’s largest water service operators, our mission is to reliably supply wholesome and clean drinking water at the customer’s tap, treat wastewater to the highest EU standards to safeguard public health, and protect our environment. Innovation, efficiency, and reliability underpin all of our business activities but so also does our awareness of the need to recover and reuse resources and our commitment to ensure that our operations activities should be environmentally sustainable, including the production of Biofert®, an organic nutrient\-rich agricultural fertiliser, much loved by farmers. Our hands\-on expertise extends from Advanced Digestion, CHP \& Energy Management, Facility Services, Utility Management, Water Metering Services, Asset Maintenance to MEICA Design \& Construction. CAW consistently delivers cost\-effective, Operations \& Maintenance solutions to Water Utility and Industrial clients, through our innovative Lean 6\-sigma approach and accredited integrated management systems. We competitively tender for O\&M and Design, Build \& Operate contracts in the municipal \& industrial sectors in Ireland and the UK. **Overview of the Project Engineer Role** We are seeking to expand our Engineering team to co\-ordinate and deliver capital works across Ringsend and other plants. These works include plant upgrades, retrofit works and capital replacement. The Project Engineer will focus on the co\-ordination of design information and site works performed by in\-house and by third party contractors. This will include working with multi\-disciplinary teams of civil, mechanical, process and electrical/ICA engineers/ contractors. The Project Engineer will be working with a team of experienced engineers and project managers to develop their knowledge and skills. The Project Engineer will be based in the CAW Engineering office at the Ringsend Wastewater Treatment Plant in Ringsend, Dublin 4\. Engineering activity across the range of projects will require the role to be fulfilled on site in Ringsend. **Typical duties comprise** \- Co\-ordinating with PSCS, PSDP, Client and other stakeholders \- Performing the duties required for the Project Co\-Ordinator for the Construction Stage \- Site supervision \& management of in\-house and external contractors \- Coordinate responses to Client requests for technical information \- Production of documentation, including reports, drawings, calculations, specifications, tender documentation, project delivery plans, H\&S plans. \- Production of project budgets and programmes \- Provide input to bids and project manage appropriate projects **Required for Project Engineer role** \- Potential candidates MUST be qualified with an Engineering degree \- MUST be fluent in English \- 3 years experience in a similar role or similar environment (site experience delivering some relevant work (in a construction type environment). \- Practical experience in co\-ordinating site activities \- Practical Health \& Safety experience in both site activities and design assessment \- Good communication and presentation skills \- Ability to work on own initiative and effectively manage \& coordinate site activities \- The ability to command respect and to create a sense of community amongst the members of the project teams (essential); \- Good knowledge of techniques for planning, monitoring and controlling projects (essential); \- Sufficient seniority and credibility to advise project teams on their projects (essential); \- The ability to find ways of solving or pre\-empting problems (essential). **Additionally candidates should** \- have good inter\-personal skills with the ability to integrate into the existing engineering team, and work alongside colleagues of other disciplines. \- have good communication skills, be computer literate and hold a valid driving licence \- be able to interact positively with senior and junior staff alike \- be progressive and imaginative in their work and strive to add value where possible for the benefit of the company and our clients

title: Qualified Domestic Plumbers & 2nd, 3rd and 4th year Apprentices
description: Due to continued expansion, Walsh and Sheehan Ltd. is seekin Domestic Qualified Plumbers and 2nd, 3rd \& 4th year Plumbing Apprentices to join our team for a ongoing project in Wexford City. This is an excellent opportunity to work with a reputable and growing company on long\-term developments in a supportive team environment. **What We’re Looking For:** ✅ Valid Manual Handling Certification (required) ✅ Safe Pass (required) ✅ Strong work ethic and commitment to high standards ✅ Ability to work independently and as part of a team ✅ Punctuality and reliability ✅ Solid understanding of domestic plumbing systems ✅ Passion and pride in your trade **Job Details:** * Job Type: Full\-time * Schedule: Day shift, Monday to Friday * Start Date: Immediate start available * Location: On\-site (Carlow) If you’re ready to advance your plumbing career with a company that values quality, teamwork, and long\-term growth, we’d love to hear from you. Job Type: Full\-time Licence/Certification: * Manual Handling Certificate (required) * Safe Pass (required) Work Location: In person

title: Quality Assurance Quality Control Engineer
description: **Junior QA/QC Engineer – Pharma Construction Projects** Dublin, Ireland \| On\-site \| Permanent We are currently partnering with a construction contractor delivering a **pharmaceutical project in Dublin** , and due to the award of new work they are looking to hire a **Junior QA/QC Engineer** to join the team. This position is ideal for someone with a **civil engineering or construction background** who wants to develop their career within **quality, compliance and project handover on large construction / pharma CAPEX projects** . Responsibilities * Support QA/QC activities on a live pharmaceutical construction project * Maintain quality documentation and site records * Assist with package handover and turnover * Work within the company quality management system * Support inspections, reporting and compliance checks * Liaise with site engineers, supervisors and project managers * Ensure works are completed in line with project quality standards Requirements * Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction, or similar * Experience on construction sites (groundworks / structural / building works preferred) * Exposure to QA / QC / Quality / Handover activities is an advantage * Pharma / life sciences project experience beneficial but not essential * Strong attention to detail and documentation skills * Willing to work on site full time Details * Location: Dublin * Type: Permanent * Level: Junior / early\-career preferred * Start date: May 2026 * On\-site role (hybrid possible later in project) 📩 Apply or contact: **aholt@optimussearch.com**

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: **About the role** The Quantity Surveyor will support the commercial management of projects across a range of sectors, working closely with ORS Project Managers, clients, and external consultants. This is an excellent opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor looking to step into a broader consultancy\-style role within a multidisciplinary environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Cost planning, estimating, and budget development * Preparation of tender documentation and evaluation of returns * Procurement support and subcontractor appointment * Preparation of valuations, cost reports, and financial forecasts * Assessment and agreement of variations and final accounts * Contract administration support and commercial risk management * Close collaboration with ORS Project Managers and client teams **Essential Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying / Construction Economics (or equivalent) * 2–5 years’ relevant post\-graduate QS experience * Experience in consultancy or contractor environments * Strong commercial and contractual awareness * Proficiency in Buildsoft/Cubit, Microsoft Excel and Cost Reporting Documents * Experience with measurement of full Bills of Quantities in accordance with the current Agreed Rules of Measurement * Excellent communication and organisational skills * Irish project experience essential **What We Can Offer You** Being part of ORS means more than just a job, it means joining a company that invests in you and your ambitions with transparency, structure and support. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join ORS, which include: * Competitive salary * Performance\-related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Remote\-first hybrid model – use your home as a base and any of our office locations as you see fit * Work from abroad * Flexible working hours * Access to over 60 health disciplines through our Kara Connect Platform * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity, paternity and fertility leave * Company and birthday annual leave days * Death in Service benefit * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture, social, wellness and CPD committees * Talent Development Programmes * Access to leading career coach Gerry Duffy **About ORS** ORS is a world\-class multidisciplinary building consultancy with more than 30 years of delivering technical excellence across the built environment. Our mission is simple but powerful: We endeavour to make the lives of our team, clients, partners and communities happier and better in the ways that really matter every single day. Don’t just take our word for it! ORS has won numerous awards, recognising our Great Place to Work culture. * Winner of the Engineering Consultancy of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025 * Winner of the Graduate Programme of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025 * Winner of the Best Company to Work For at the Irish Design \& Build Awards 2024 \& 2025 * Winner of the Best People Development of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2024 * Winner of the Recruitment \& Retention Strategy at the Workplace Excellence Awards 2024 * Great Place to Work certified consecutively since 2017 * Top 3 Company Best Workplaces in the medium\-sized category at the Great Place to Work awards 2025 * Best Workplaces for Health \& Wellbeing 2025 Great Place to Work * Engineers Ireland CPD Accreditation since 20217 * Awarded for Hybrid Way of Working at the 2022 Great Place to Work Awards * Silver Accreditation through Investors in Diversity 2025 ORS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive company culture. We foster a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects and supports their individual strengths and experiences.

title: Quantity Surveyor - 2026 Work Placement
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin or Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Procurement \& Cost Management team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. A role in the Procurement and Cost management team will include liaising with other multi\-disciplinary contributors within Arup for the successful outcome of real\-world projects. You will be required to deliver projects to the required technical standards in a timely manner, reporting to the team leader or project manager as appropriate. **As a Quantity Surveyor work placement student at Arup, you will:** * Assist senior team members in delivering construction projects dealing with payment certificates and claims. * Assist senior team members in the preparation of bills of quantities, ensuring compliance with required method of measurement. * Be proactive in communicating and working with the direct team as well as Arup colleagues from various disciplines for the successful delivery of tasks. * Follow instructions and/or use own initiative to achieve designated tasks in a timely manner. * Understand the required outputs/deliverables of a project or assignment and work efficiently to deliver them. * Develop relationships with colleagues, always being professional, presentable and articulate. * Adopt a flexible approach to working, supporting multiple teams across the buildings and infrastructure sectors **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for students who are currently working towards RICS accredited, surveying\-based degree. We also value students who are: * Proactive, motivated and enthusiastic. Able to apply their own initiative to undertake tasks independently. * A team player, who can adopt flexible working practices to support the needs of the business. * Articulate, with both excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Logical in their approach to tasks, with the ability to interpret, understand and analyse data. * Output focused, always working with the objective in mind. * Commercially astute with strong numerical skills, demonstrating an attention to detail. * Proficient with Microsoft Office products and ideally Cost X (Although not necessary). **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Rack Decomissioning Technician , DCC Communities, DECOM
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- The Decommissioning Technical team is responsible for processing and facilitating large scale legacy Data Center equipment through a liquidation process to ensure that targeted equipment is ready to be removed off premises. Team members will also process large volumes of customer data to ensure that all customer data is sanitized and properly managed. This position requires candidates to be a self\-starter, highly motivated individual who can adhere to strict processes and be able to perform repetitive tasks with no deviation and demonstrate continuously consistent results. Team Members will be working in a team environment not only with other members of the team but also from other teams and must be able to communicate and promote cross\-team functionality. This is an entry level position and qualified candidates do not require intimate details of software operations or networking protocols. Amazon Data Centers takes customer data with the utmost importance, and selected candidates must be able to perform repetitive tasks without variance and adhere to strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that ensures customer data is protected and properly handled. Key job responsibilities Data Center Technicians (Decommission Technician) will be responsible for: Decommissioning and processing of legacy hardware out of the data center facilities Moving/lifting packages usually no greater than 75 lbs. Unpacking/repacking fragile electronics Relocating and moving of large enterprise equipment Creating/attaching labels and barcodes Hard drive installation and removal, and destruction Basic Networking removal/installation and clearing of configurations. Physical layer and Application layer Updating progress through a web ticketing system Reporting of processes and regular status updates Documenting procedures Escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required Familiarity with basic PC components/hardware: * HDD/SSD/Flash/Removable storage media * USB/FireWire/Esata * Ram/CPU/Motherboard/Input\-Output devices There may be an element of shift based work required with this role. A day in the life AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. About the team About AWS Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * High school or equivalent diploma * Experience with computer hardware troubleshooting and repair * Experience in networking **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Experience dealing effectively with customers during problem resolution and operating efficiently under pressure Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Recruitment Executive – Career Change Opportunity
description: **Recruitment Executive – Career Change Opportunity** * **This wasn’t the plan long\-term, was it?** * **You’ve learned a lot, but you want more from your career** * **More progression. More recognition. More return for your effort** At Access, we specialise in turning high\-performing people from operational backgrounds into top recruiters and business developers. With a structured training programme, we’ve built a track record of internal growth \- every leader in the business started at an entry\-level role , and we’ve delivered 50–100% year\-on\-year growth for the past 6 years . We’re looking for professionals from **Logistics, Supply Chain, or Warehouse** environments (1–2 years) to transition into a **Recruitment role** . If you’re used to managing priorities and keeping things moving, this is your opportunity to move into a more dynamic, people\-focused role where your performance drives your progression and earnings. **What you’ll gain:** * **A career that grows with you** — instead of keeping you in the same place * **Earnings that reflect your determination** — helping you build the life you actually want * **Progression** that changes your day\-to\-day, your confidence, and your options * **Real stories of people who made the same move** — and didn’t look back **What we look for:** * Has 6 months to 2 years of experience in Logistics, Supply Chain, or Warehouse roles (e.g., Logistics Coordinator, Warehouse Coordinator, Operations Assistant, Stock Controller) * Ambitious in a steady, realistic way — wanting more over time * Ready to take a step that changes your trajectory **Additional Information** This is a full\-time, office\-based role in Dublin City Centre, Monday to Friday, in a business that values ownership, long\-term client relationships, and meaningful commercial impact. In your first 2 years, consultants can expect to earn an average of €64K\+ OTE, with accelerated progression for those who demonstrate strong client ownership and consistent results. For more information, contact Ines Duarte confidentially at \+353 1 572 118 1 or email ines.duarte@accesshealthcare.ie.

title: Reward Partner
description: **About The Job** Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. People \& Culture at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Do As a Reward Partner** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Leadership: Leads, advises or guides more junior members of the reward team. * Client Management \& Stakeholders: Accountable for client group/s and first point\-of\-contact for P\&C community within client group/s. Proactively and continuously develops strong and constructive relationships. Monitors and responds to emails. Implements solutions by coordinating with other departments such as Payroll, Technology and other P\&C colleagues. Proactively and continuously develops strong and constructive relationships. * Policy \& Processes: Interprets global policies and ensures their effective application for client group, resolving the client issue. Rarely, and in cases of non\-resolution, escalates challenges to policy. Develops recommendations for new or existing global policies. Provides guidance to P\&C Colleagues on the correct application of policy. Coordinates and tracks all reward related processes for client group/s. Ensures all processes within own area of accountability are clearly documented as a shared resource and are always up to date. * Reward Cycles: Ensures the overall success of the reward cycle by running the review processes for client group/s, ensuring full synchronisation with peers and overall deadlines. Contributes to all elements of the process including planning, tracking and mitigants. * Communication: Independently drafts and occasionally delivers high quality written communication including decks that encompass analysis and narrative. Ensures that all communication is clear and accurate enabling the client group to make appropriate decisions. All queries are responded to effectively. * Analysis: Creates or reviews high quality reports. Models the costing, budgeting, forecasting and reporting for client group/s. Assists the delivery of all statutory reporting requirements including calculations, analysis and presentation. Independently suggests and prepares reward options for client group/s. Evaluates or levels jobs and explains job architecture rationale to client group. * External Analysis: Keeps up to date with all market trends and relevant legislation and advises client group as appropriate. Creates and advises on pay ranges and bonus structures. Provides ad\-hoc advice on market rates for roles. Completes the survey submission to external benchmarking providers. * Operational \& Vendors: Undertakes all operational requirements within role and as and when required. Works closely with third party vendors within agreed service level. Contacts vendors on routine matters to resolve queries. Sets up POs and administers invoices and related activity. Prepares budget for external vendors. Other: Performs all other reward duties and projects as assigned from time to time. Anticipates hurdles and suggests mitigants and solutions. Proactively suggests changes to policy and practice to improve the client experience, enhance accuracy and ensure strategic alignment. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Highly numerate and analytical with strong interpersonal and planning skills * Solution focused, attention to detail and right first\-time attitude * Exceptional verbal and written communication ability to explain aspects of reward in a clear, compliant and risk\-free manner * Demonstrated ability to build rapport with internal clients and other colleagues * Collaborative approach, effectively working with others to achieve team success * Ability to plan, schedule and arrange own activities to accomplish objectives * A flexible approach and adapting of role to meet current and future business needs * Takes on new tasks as required and demonstrates flexibility and willingness to adapt to change. * Seeks out trends in the reward arena including industry networking * Has a solid understanding of all Total Reward policies and principles * Advanced capability in using MS Office (PowerPoint, Word and Excel) * Strong general IT and Digital awareness * Exceptional knowledge of the regulatory, tax or compliance rules governing key aspects of reward and stays current with developments in these areas * Strong relationship management skills including the proven ability to quickly build strong personal networks with clients and peers. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-5595

title: SC Engineering Graduate
description: Job Description * **Role Title \& Team** + Graduate Process systems engineer + Team: Process engineering + Reports to: Engineering and continuous improvement manager * **About the Role** + The successful candidate would be expected to perform across the below areas of the business + To play a leading role in a dynamic Operations team delivering daily support to the depot Managers. + To work with a state of the art Warehouse Management Systems (WMS). + To deliver best in class service to the business in order to minimize the impact of downtime to daily operations. + To identify opportunities to improve the processes and systems that drive the day to day operation in all Uniphar facilities + The role will be centred on providing continuous improvement and process optimization throughout the warehouse, including but not limited to, the application and all its associated processes, workflows and functionalities while minimising\\removing all non\-value\-added activities. + Lead Internal projects/cross departmental projects focused on meeting quick deadlines while delivering a high\-quality output. Establishing metrics to monitor the performance of each project and understanding the benefit to the organization. * **Rotations** (if applicable) + This role may involve up to three rotations across areas including Supply Chain, Commercial Operations, or Quality. Final rotation assignments will align to business needs. Each rotation will include clear responsibilities, a learning plan, and project deliverables. * **What You Will Learn \& Deliver** + Build foundational knowledge of systems, tools, and processes. \- Deliver assigned reports and tasks accurately and on time. \- Contribute to team projects and process improvements. \- Objectives will be defined during onboarding and refined as priorities evolve. * **Required Skills \& Knowledge** + Data analysis skills + Experience across the MS suite of applications + Some SAP/systems knowledge would be preferable + Clear communication skills. + Organised with ability to manage tasks and meet deadlines. + Eligibility to work in the relevant jurisdiction. * **Education \& Qualifications** + Minimum Honors Degree in Engineering or Science related course + Proficient in all Microsoft packages, AutoCAD, SQL knowledge an advantage + Candidate must be team orientated with the ability to work in cross\-functional teams to accomplish goals set by the business. + Results orientated – must be able to take projects to completion + Quality Orientated + Strong numerical \& analytical skills Job Description**Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: SRE - Site Reliability Engineer
description: **About Keyrock** Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change\-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation. Today, we rock with over 240 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 45 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, Singapore and Paris, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight. We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well\-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high\-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks. But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives. At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it. **Mission statement** **Central Infrastructure Team** CIT at Keyrock is responsible for keeping all production systems running smoothly By leveraging cloud technologies and platform automation to design, build and maintain highly reliable and scalable systems. We focus on continuously improving the reliability of scalable software systems in accordance with the keyrock internal architecture. As SREs at the Central Infrastructure Team we help the Engineering team balance speed and stability at scale. **Job Description** We are looking for an experienced Site Reliability Engineer to join our Central Infrastructure Team, focusing on AWS, Kubernetes, and modern DevSecOps best practices. This role involves designing, implementing, and maintaining highly scalable and resilient cloud infrastructure to support our trading operations. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in cloud computing, automation, and CI/CD pipelines, ensuring high availability and performance for mission\-critical systems. **Key Responsibilities** * Cloud Infrastructure Management: Design, deploy, and maintain scalable and resilient infrastructure on AWS using Infrastructure\-as\-Code (IaC). * Kubernetes Administration: Manage and optimize Kubernetes clusters for containerized applications, ensuring high availability and security. * Automation \& CI/CD: Implement and manage CI/CD pipelines for efficient deployment, testing, and monitoring of applications. * Observability \& Monitoring: Develop comprehensive monitoring solutions using Prometheus, Grafana, LGTM stack, or similar tools to improve system reliability. * Security \& Compliance: Apply best practices for cloud security, IAM policies, and compliance frameworks (SOC2, ISO 27001, etc.). * Incident Response \& Performance Optimization: Troubleshoot issues, perform root cause analysis, and implement fixes to optimize performance. * Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Utilize Terraform, Ansible, or similar tools to automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration management. * Collaboration \& Knowledge Sharing: Work closely with software engineering, architecture and security teams to promote DevOps culture and best practices. * Disaster Recovery \& Reliability Engineering: Design failover and backup strategies to ensure business continuity in the event of failures. **Background And Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. * 5\+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure, SRE, or DevOps roles. * Interest in or any exposure to trading or similar themes would be desirable (not essential) * AWS Certified SysOps Administrator \- Associate: desired. **Competences and personality** * Strong expertise in AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, VPC, IAM, etc.). * Hands\-on experience with Kubernetes (EKS, K3s, or self\-managed clusters). * Proficiency in scripting and automation using Python, Bash, or similar. * Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation, or Ansible). * Familiarity with monitoring, logging, and observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, etc.). * Strong understanding of networking concepts (VPC, Load Balancers, DNS, Firewalls). * Experience with DevOps methodologies, CI/CD pipelines, and GitOps practices. * Experience with high\-performance and low\-latency (sub millisecond) systems. * Familiarity with serverless architectures and event\-driven computing. * Familiarity with Rust compilation processes and techniques. * Willing to collaborate and communicate asynchronously. * Actively look for opportunities to improve the availability and performance of the system by applying the learnings from monitoring and observation * Team spirit, ownership, critical thinking * Exposure to cloud cost optimization and FinOps strategies. * Previous exposure working with Crypto, Traditional Finance (Trad Fi) or Trading would be highly desirable but not essential **Our recruitment philosophy** We value self\-awareness and powerful communication skills in our recruitment process. We seek fiercely passionate people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, acts with ownership and delivers solid, scalable outcomes. **Our offer** * A competitive salary package, with various benefits depending on method of engagement (Employee vs Contractor) * Autonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely * The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence * Continuing Professional Development plan with learning and certification path in accordance with both the team objectives and areas of interests As an employer we are committed to building a positive and collaborative work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, and hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance. Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock. https://keyrock.com/careers/
