title: Sales Development Representative
description: **Are you ready to embark on a career that truly affects people around the world? Trulioo invites you to be a catalyst for change in the dynamic realm of digital identity verification. As the global front\-runner in our industry, we are redefining how businesses grow, innovate and comply online.** Picture yourself at the forefront of innovation, contributing to our award\-winning platform that enables organizations worldwide to quickly onboard customers, optimize costs and combat fraud. Fueled by Silicon Valley support, Trulioo stands as the trusted platform that can verify more than 5 billion people and 700 million business entities spanning 195 countries. But Trulioo is more than a tech company. We are a united force of dedicated experts committed to establishing trust online \- and we’re proud to be recognized as a **BC Top Employer for the second consecutive year** , reflecting our commitment to an inclusive, collaborative, and people\-first workplace. Headquartered in Vancouver and with strategic hubs in San Diego and Dublin, we foster a culture of collaboration and open communication. Our offices support a hybrid model and staff typically work three days per week at a hub location. Join us where excitement meets innovation and contribute to a world where trust and technology unite. **What We Offer** * Comprehensive Benefits: We provide a robust benefits package for full\-time, permanent employees, including health, dental, and vision coverage, retirement plans with company match, paid time off, parental leave, and an annual education \& training stipend (equivalent to $1,000 in local currency). Specific benefits may vary by location and will be discussed further during the interview process. * Flexible Hybrid Working Environment: Our offices are designed to support both collaboration and flexibility. Enjoy weekly lunches, quality coffee, and regular social events. Many locations also feature parent rooms, on\-site gyms, comfortable lounges, and adaptable workstations to support your comfort and productivity. * Wellness: We care about your well\-being. Team members have access to wellness workshops and events, as well as a complimentary Headspace subscription to help you stay focused, grounded, and energized. * Employee Resource Groups: Belonging is an important part of doing your best work. Our ERGs provide an inclusive space, support and community for employees of diverse backgrounds and allies. We host informative, fun sessions and celebrations that are often open to the entire organization. **Position Summary** Reporting to the Manager, Global Sales Development, the **Sales Development Representative (SDR)** is a key player in driving revenue growth for the company. This role is focused on leading the charge in identifying, engaging, and nurturing high\-potential prospects. You will be responsible for developing strategies and executing outreach campaigns that align with company goals and market trends. As a member of the Revenue team, you will also contribute to process improvements and collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure alignment and success. **What You'll Be Doing** * Conduct thorough market research on targeted accounts to identify key contacts and roles that align with our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP). * Develop and execute personalized outreach strategies to engage key decision\-makers at prospective accounts. * Utilize Salesforce to create, manage, and analyze account lists, re\-engaging stale leads and driving them back into the sales funnel. * Lead a dedicated outbound account sequence, incorporating multi\-channel communication methods including calls, emails, video, and social media. * Mentor and guide junior SDRs, sharing best practices and ensuring high standards of outreach and communication. * Collaborate closely with the marketing, SDR, and sales teams to ensure consistent messaging and alignment of goals. * Maintain impeccable data hygiene within Salesforce CRM, ensuring all client interactions and details are accurately recorded and up\-to\-date. * Transition qualified leads to Account Executives (AEs) smoothly, providing detailed context to ensure a seamless continuation of the sales process. * Master our tech stack, including Salesforce, Salesloft, Apollo, Sales Navigator, Gong and other tools to maximize efficiency and effectiveness. * Stay ahead of industry trends and continuously improve outreach strategies based on insights and feedback. **What You'll Bring** * 2\+ years of demonstrated success in a sales development role, with a strong track record of building and maintaining a robust sales pipeline, especially with outbound outreach. * Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to distill complex ideas into clear, actionable messages. * Strategic thinker with the ability to quickly adapt messaging to various industries, roles, and stages of the sales cycle and articulate the value proposition of our platform as well as key customer stories. * Proven leadership and mentorship experience, with the ability to inspire and guide junior team members. * Strong time management skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Effective at prospect planning \& understanding how to collaborate effectively with their respective AEs; * Tech\-savvy with a deep understanding of CRM systems, sales outreach platforms, and social media tools. * Experience in a fast\-paced startup environment is a plus. * Resilient and adaptable, with a strong ability to overcome obstacles and maintain a positive attitude. * Solution\-oriented mindset, with a focus on identifying customer needs and developing tailored solutions to drive business growth. * Coachable and has a passion for continuous learning and self\-improvement, with a proactive approach to staying updated on industry trends and best practices. **It’s A Bonus If You Have...** * Experience working with Salesforce and other advanced CRM/sales outreach platforms. * Extensive outbound sales experience, particularly in engaging and converting enterprise\-level accounts. * Strong problem\-solving skills, with a track record of overcoming sales challenges and driving results. * An innovative thinker who thrives in a dynamic sales landscape, always looking for new ways to connect with and win over prospects. **Thriving at Trulioo** At Trulioo, you’re not just an employee. You’re a valued member of our Trulicrew on a journey of professional and personal growth with a world\-class organization. With Trulioo, you have the power to revolutionize the intersection of technology, digital trust and online identity to open the global economy to everyone. Together, we can shape the digital future. We’re on the lookout for exceptional people to empower with trust, autonomy and the freedom to cultivate their potential. Your curiosity, meticulous attention to detail and passion to contribute are highly valued. If that resonates with you, apply today to become a part of our team. Join us in shaping a future where your career isn’t just a journey but also a boundless exploration into the possibilities of technology and digital identity verification. Get an inside look at life at Trulioo by following us on LinkedIn, or learn more about us at www.trulioo.com *If you don’t see yourself fully reflected in every job requirement listed on the posting above, we still encourage you to reach out and apply. Research has shown that minorities and underrepresented groups often only apply when they feel 100% qualified. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive and diverse company and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and life experiences to apply.* *Trulioo welcomes applications from people with disabilities. Support is available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. Finally, we know from time\-to\-time emergencies happen and you may need to reschedule an interview \- we understand and encourage you to be in communication without worrying about losing the opportunity or your credibility.* **Privacy Notice** As part of our job application process, Trulioo collects, processes, and discloses personal data for the purpose of identifying suitable candidates for our job openings. For more detail, please visit Trulioo’s Website Privacy Policy in the section “When you apply for a job”.

title: School Meal Cover - County Offaly
description: **A Little Bit About Us** Looking for a job that actually makes a difference \- and gives you something new every day? At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help kids build healthy habits for life. We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are! **So, what’s the job?** We’re on the hunt for **School Meals Cover Staff** \- reliable, adaptable people who can jump into action at short notice and keep things running like clockwork. This isn’t your typical 9 \- 5\. You’ll be our on\-the\-ground hero, stepping into different schools as needed (when our staff in the schools are absent), bringing the smiles, and making sure no child goes hungry. If you’re looking for routine and predictability, this might not be for you. But if you like variety, enjoy being out and about, this could be your perfect fit \- keeping you on your toes and feeling young! Just like our regular school meals workers, you will be the smiley face representing us in the school. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the schools. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven and then distributing them to classrooms. If there’s no need for staff cover on a given day, you’ll head out to schools to carry out simple (but effective) audits. Our goal is always to provide the best possible service to children \- and that includes staying on top of hygiene standards and following all HACCP and EHO guidelines. If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you: * Go into a new work environment each day with enthusiasm and confidence. * Load pre\-packaged school meals into ovens. * Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines. * Unload ovens and distribute lunches to classrooms. * Ensure adequate stock levels. * Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule. * Maintain temperature and cleaning records. * Restock the fruit bowls in each classroom. * Distribute snacks to classrooms. * Conduct audits in schools to ensure compliance with EHO requirements. * HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided. **What we’re looking for** Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers \- people who are sound, good\-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal. You’ll need to be a quick learner, great with people, and happy to go with the flow. A full driving licence and your own transport are essential \- some schools might be just around the corner, others could be a bit of a spin. But that’s part of the fun \- you’ll get to know every back road and coffee spot in your area! We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every school in Ireland has a lift (just yet)! Working with children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly. If you’ve worked with kids or food before, great. If not, don’t let it put you off \- we value people from all backgrounds and we’ll show you the ropes. All you need is the ability to read English, follow instructions, and heat each lunch with care. **I still have questions actually…** No problem! We’re here to answer them. * When will I work? You will need to be available **Monday to Friday from 7 am to 3\.30pm** . Your precise hours per day will depend on the school’s requirements. * Will I have to work all year long? No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well\-deserved rest! * What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag? Well, for starters \- a decent rate of pay and a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back. The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms. And best of all? You get to be part of a lovely school environment, surrounded by friendly faces (and the occasional tiny comedian in a uniform). * How much is it paid? It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn **€580 per week** , and we’ll also cover your expenses for using your own car

title: School Meal Worker- Gaelscoil Eanna 19725C
description: **A Little Bit About Us** Looking for a job that actually makes a difference \- and fits into your day\-to\-day routine? At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help kids build healthy habits for life. We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are! **So, what’s the job?** We’re on the hunt for **School Meals Workers** – reliable, cheerful folks who take pride in doing things right and bring a bit of warmth to every school day. Think of yourself as the final link in the lunch chain – the friendly face everyone sees, and the front\-line hero representing us in the school. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the schools. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven and then distributing them to classrooms. If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you: * Load pre\-packaged school meals into ovens. * Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines. * Unload ovens and distribute lunches to classrooms. * Ensure adequate stock levels. * Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule. * Maintain temperature and cleaning records. * Restock the fruit bowls in each classroom. * Distribute snacks to classrooms. * HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided. **What we’re looking for** Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers \- people who are sound, good\-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal. We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every school in Ireland has a lift (just yet)! Working with children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly. If you’ve worked with kids or food before, great. If not, don’t let it put you off \- we value people from all backgrounds and we’ll show you the ropes. All you need is the ability to read English, follow instructions, and heat each lunch with care. **I still have questions actually…** No problem! We’re here to answer them. * When will I work? This job is offered on a part\-time basis, **2 hours a day, Thursday \& Friday** ! * Will I have to work all year long? No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well\-deserved rest! * What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag? Well, for starters \- a decent rate of pay and a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back. The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms. And best of all? You get to be part of a lovely school environment, surrounded by friendly faces (and the occasional tiny comedian in a uniform). * How much is it paid? It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn **€14\.65 per hour** .

title: Securities & Derivatives Intermediate Analyst
description: Are you looking for a career move that will put you at the heart of a global financial institution? Then bring your skills in analysis, problem solving and communication to Citi’s Middle Office Oversight team team Shape your career with Citi in Dublin. By joining Citi, you will become part of a global organisation whose mission is to serve as a trusted partner to our clients by responsibly providing financial services that enable growth and economic progress. Citi has had a presence in Ireland since 1965, it was one of the first foreign banks to open an office in the country and is the Citibank Europe Plc Headquarters. **Team / Role Overview:** This is an Intermediate level position within the Middle Office Oversight team in Dublin. The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring all OTC (Over the Counter) Derivative transactions originating from client trading desks are processed. The overall objective of this role is to assist in the clearance of any transaction capture exceptions, assisting with OTC cash settlement and trade confirmations and the general investigation of client securities and derivatives transaction issues as they arise. **What you will do:** * Assist with the investigation and resolution of any trade capture issues that may arise, ensuring that all client OTC bookings are captured successfully each day. * Ensure all OTC trade bookings are fully confirmed to with the counterparties, leveraging Markit Trade Manager * Approve and process all bilateral OTC cash settlements and adhere to all Manual Payment Processing (MPP) guidelines. * Coordinate transaction processing issues to the appropriate department and collaborate on a solution * Perform daily reconciliation processes and investigate all breaks that arise thoroughly * Actively participate on recurring service calls, both internally and with our clients. Escalate matters promptly and see all issues through to resolution. * Perform regulatory controls, account reconciliations, and record retention activities in accordance with established policies * Prepare and submit periodic internal and external reporting based on the department’s activities for the month. **What we'll need from you:** * Relevant fund administration experience preferred * Fundamental understanding of OTC derivative products such as swaps and options. * Proven ability to perform various concurrent activities/projects in a high\-risk environment * Experience with payment processing or transaction processing/confirmation an advantage * Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills **What we can offer you:** By joining Citi Dublin, you will not only be part of a business casual workplace with a hybrid working model (up to 2 days working at home per week), but also receive a competitive base salary (which is annually reviewed) and enjoy a whole host of additional benefits that support you (and your family) to be well, live well and save well. Alongside these benefits Citi is committed to ensuring our workplace is where everyone feels comfortable coming to work as their whole self every day. We want the best talent around the world to be energised to join us, motivated to stay, and empowered to thrive. **Sounds like Citi has everything you need? Then apply to discover the true extent of your capabilities.** \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Job Family Group:** Operations \- Transaction Services \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Job Family:** Securities and Derivatives Processing \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Time Type:** Full time \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Primary Location Full Time Salary Range:** €52 580,00 \- €73 620,00 \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Most Relevant Skills** Please see the requirements listed above. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Other Relevant Skills** For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **Automated Processing and AI** We use automated processing, including artificial intelligence, for our legitimate business interests (or our reasonable and appropriate business purposes) to identify and align the candidate's skills and abilities with a specific job opening. Additionally, if you so choose, or consent, we can match your skills and abilities to other suitable roles at Citi. Importantly, all our hiring processes and decisions, including determining your suitability for a role, are conducted, checked, and decided by individuals. Our automated processing and AI do not involve relying on automatic or autonomous decision\-making. Please refer to any Jurisdictional Considerations, with specific provisions for your country (where relevant) for further details. \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- *This job opening is for an existing job vacancy.* \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- *Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic protected by law.* *If you are a person with a disability and need a reasonable accommodation to use our search tools and/or apply for a career opportunity review* *Accessibility at Citi.* *View Citi’s EEO Policy Statement and the Know Your Rights poster.*

title: Service Desk Analyst - Entry Level
description: **Presidio, Where Teamwork and Innovation Shape the Future** At Presidio, we’re at the forefront of a global technology revolution, transforming industries through cutting\-edge digital solutions and next\-generation AI. We empower businesses—and their customers—to achieve more through innovation, automation, and intelligent insights. **The Role** This is a 1st level position providing support on customer end user infrastructure level as well as acting as the first point of contact for any issues or requests, via phone, email or our ticketing system. **Responsibilities Include** * Answering the phones and collecting information on issue/request. * Scoping tickets logged via email or ticketing system. * Understanding prioritisation as per ITIL Model. * Creating / modifying tickets to ensure information such as Type, Technology, Priority is correct before escalating to the correct team. * Working and resolving on Service Requests and Incidents from an End User perspective. * Customer communication via emails and phones. * Acting as the first point of contact for the entire Managed Services department. **Required Skills And Professional Experience** * ITIL process experience * Process management experience * Dealing with customers over phone and email **Preferred Skills And Professional Experience** * Drive to deliver outstanding customer service * Ability to communicate well with all levels of staff at client sites. * Quickly build rapport and gain client confidence. * Work individually and as a team. * Be able to follow company procedures and methods. * Available to work out of hours / weekends if necessary. * Be a logical thinker. * Have a proven track record in trouble shooting and problem solving. * Be able to utilise all tools available to resolve 95% of issues at the first time of asking. * Ability to quickly learn new products **Your future at Presidio** Joining Presidio means stepping into a culture of trailblazers—thinkers, builders, and collaborators—who push the boundaries of what’s possible. With our expertise in AI\-driven analytics, cloud solutions, cybersecurity, and next\-gen infrastructure, we enable businesses to stay ahead in an ever\-evolving digital world. Here, your impact is real. Whether you're harnessing the power of Generative AI, architecting resilient digital ecosystems, or driving data\-driven transformation, you’ll be part of a team that is shaping the future. **Ready to innovate? Let’s redefine what’s next—together.** **About Presidio** At Presidio, speed and quality meet technology and innovation. Presidio is a trusted ally for organizations across industries with a decades\-long history of building traditional IT foundations and deep expertise in AI and automation, security, networking, digital transformation, and cloud computing. Presidio fills gaps, removes hurdles, optimizes costs, and reduces risk. Presidio’s expert technical team develops custom applications, provides managed services, enables actionable data insights and builds forward\-thinking solutions that drive strategic outcomes for clients globally. **For more information, visit www.presidio.com** **.** Presidio is committed to hiring the most qualified candidates to join our amazing culture. We aim to attract and hire top talent from all backgrounds, including underrepresented and marginalized communities. We encourage women, people of color, people with disabilities, and veterans to apply for open roles at Presidio. Diversity of skills and thought is a key component to our business success. ***Recruitment Agencies, Please Note:*** Presidio does not accept unsolicited agency resumes/CVs. Do not forward resumes/CVs to our careers email address, Presidio employees or any other means. Presidio is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/CVs.

title: Shop Floor Assistant - Full time
description: **Floor Staff \- Full Time** ***Please note; Pay is scaled to experience.*** **ABOUT THE JOB** EEC Hardware is currently looking for additional members for our Floor Staff. This role is social and all applicants are expected to maintain a high standard of customer service as they interact with the public. A strong willingness to learn is a major advantage as the role will often require knowledge of a vast variety of products to better assist customers. The ideal candidate will have a history of organisation, as the management of product stock levels demands a eye for detail. **General responsibilities would be as follows;** * Assisting customers face to face, using a generalised knowledge of store layout and products. * The use of our ERP / Till system to process purchases. * The maintaining of goods inwards onto the shop floor, stocking, laying out, and pricing products. * Ensuring a tidy, clean and organised work enviroment. As the role is **Full\-Time ONLY** and **NOT entry\-level,** previous sales experience is essential with all new employees receiving training on our systems. This role offers progression, towards the management of own sections through stock upkeep and towards our trade counter as a Sales Assistant. **WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** We're looking for a motivated, organised and sales driven candidate that will actively assist customers in a friendly manner. The ideal candidate would proactively learn the stock to better assist customers, even becoming familiar with regulars to better assist any inquiries. **Essential** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills for assisting customers. * A strong ability to work well under pressure during busy seasons. * A high level of organisation and attention to detail for the maintaining of stock. * Basic Computer Literacy \- (general computer usage for processing transactions) **Desired** * Relevant experience in a similar role * Experience organising stock and handling goods inwards to the shelf. * Experience assisting difficult customers, helping to resolve their issue. **ABOUT US** EEC Hardware is a hardware and homeware store situated in Balbriggan. We have our own Blueberry Hill Café that offers our Employees discounted, fresh, and high quality food. Employees will be given uniforms designed to handle a busy and often dirty enviroment, and also offers free parking for employees. We operate with a zero tolerance to bullying in the workplace in order to maintain a safe, productive work environment. **If you have any questions, please contact us at marketing@eechardware.ie** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €14\.15 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Store discount Ability to commute/relocate: * Balbriggan, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you available to work full time? Experience: * Retail: 1 year (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Snowflake Data Analyst (Contract)
description: **Snowflake Data Analyst \- 6\-Month Daily Rate Contract** **Job Description:** Our client, a leading organisation operating within financial services, is seeking an experienced Snowflake Data Analyst to support the delivery of high\-quality data insights and reporting solutions on a 6\-month daily rate contract. **Job Responsibilities** * Develop, refine and maintain advanced SQL queries within Snowflake to support reporting and analytical requirements. * Design and manage data structures including tables, views and curated datasets to ensure reliable and scalable reporting. * Translate business requirements into clearly defined metrics, calculations and data definitions. * Perform detailed data validation, investigating discrepancies and resolving data quality issues. * Partner with data and reporting teams to deliver reusable, well\-structured datasets for dashboards and analysis. * Apply performance optimisation techniques and promote best practice usage of Snowflake across analytical workloads. * Contribute to documentation of data models, logic and processes to support transparency and governance. **Experience Required** * Minimum 3\+ years’ experience in a data analyst or similar analytical role. * Strong SQL expertise with hands\-on experience using Snowflake or a comparable cloud\-based data warehouse. * Proven experience designing and maintaining data models to support business reporting. * Experience engaging with stakeholders to translate business queries into effective data solutions. * Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve data inconsistencies in complex datasets. **Educational Requirements** * Third level degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems or a related discipline. **HOW TO APPLY:** * If you are interested in this role, please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Snowflake Intelligence Analyst
description: **Snowflake Intelligence Analyst \- 6\-Month Daily Rate Contract** **Job Description:** Our client, a leading organisation operating within the data and analytics space, is seeking a ***Snowflake Intelligence*** Analyst to design and optimise data solutions that enable natural language querying and AI\-driven insights across the business. **Job Responsibilities** * Design, configure and optimise Snowflake data structures, including tables, views and data models, to support AI\-enabled querying and analytics. * Define, structure and document business metrics, calculations and filters to ensure clarity, consistency and usability within AI\-driven solutions. * Develop, review and maintain high\-quality SQL code supporting reporting, analytics and intelligent data services. * Validate and refine AI\-generated queries and outputs, ensuring alignment with defined business logic and data standards. * Collaborate with data analysts and business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into scalable data models and AI\-ready configurations. * Monitor data quality, query performance and user feedback, implementing continuous improvements to enhance insight accuracy and coverage. * Contribute to best practice standards across analytics, modelling and AI\-enabled data consumption. **Experience Required** * Minimum 3\+ years’ experience in a data analytics, business intelligence or data engineering role. * Strong SQL expertise with hands\-on experience in Snowflake or a comparable cloud\-based data platform. * Proven experience in data modelling and supporting analytical reporting requirements. * Demonstrated experience working closely with business users to define metrics and reporting logic. * **Experience in either:** * Snowflake Intelligence, natural language\-to\-SQL tools or AI\-driven data agents, **or** * Applying AI / machine learning concepts within analytics or data\-focused products. **Educational Requirements** * Third level degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems or a related discipline. **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply with your updated CV.

title: Software Developer
description: **Job Summary** We are seeking a passionate, collaborative, and motivated **Mid\-Junior Software Engineer** to join our fintech client’s Payment Networks team on a **contract basis**. This role focuses on building **innovative, secure, and reliable transaction processing and switching solutions**, forming a critical part of the client’s core payment infrastructure. This is an excellent opportunity for someone early in their career who is eager to learn, contribute to real\-world systems, and work within an agile development environment. * **Contract Duration:** 12–24 months * **Visa Sponsorship:** Not available * **Work Authorization:** Candidates must have valid work authorization and be able to work without visa support until **May 2028** **Key Responsibilities** * Develop, test, implement, and maintain application software following established standards, processes, and best practices * Build **high\-quality, secure, and scalable software solutions** based on technical specifications * Support the development of applications, components, and system integrations * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to meet project deadlines and deliverables * Identify issues proactively and recommend effective solutions * Participate in **production support and incident management** * Create and maintain documentation such as user guides and development documentation * Continuously identify opportunities to improve processes and engineering standards **About You** * Early\-career engineering experience (internships or 1–3 years preferred) * Interest in **high\-volume, low\-latency systems** (e.g., payments, trading, real\-time systems) * Exposure to **CI/CD pipelines** (e.g., Jenkins) * Understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) practices * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A proactive mindset with a willingness to learn and grow **Preferred Skills** * Exposure to **event\-driven systems** (e.g., Kafka, NATS) * Familiarity with **testing frameworks** (e.g., JUnit, GTest, mocking frameworks) * Basic understanding of **microservices architecture** and REST APIs * Experience or coursework involving **Java, Go, or C\+\+** * Awareness of **cloud\-native development** and Linux environments * Familiarity with tools such as **Docker, Kubernetes, or Cloud Foundry** * Understanding of CI/CD\-based deployments Job Types: Full\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 12\-24 months Pay: Up to €65,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * Private medical insurance Application question(s): * Are you title: Sous Chef
description: **About the Role** We are looking for a strong, reliable, and experienced Sous Chef to join our kitchen team in a busy restaurant in Dublin City Centre. This role is ideal for a chef who can take responsibility, support kitchen operations, and run service when required. You will work closely with the kitchen team to ensure high\-quality food, good presentation, and smooth service during busy periods. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the day\-to\-day running of the kitchen * Prepare, cook, and present dishes to a high standard * Help manage kitchen operations during service * Ensure food is prepared and served on time * Maintain high standards of hygiene, HACCP, and food safety * Support stock control, ordering, and kitchen organisation * Assist with menu preparation, food trials, and plating * Help train and support junior kitchen staff * Ensure the kitchen is clean, organised, and running efficiently **Requirements** * Previous experience as a Sous Chef or strong Chef de Partie * Ability to work in a fast\-paced kitchen * Strong cooking and plating skills * Good understanding of HACCP and food safety * Ability to work under pressure and lead during service * Reliable, punctual, and professional * Flexibility to work evenings and weekends **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate * Opportunity to be part of a new and growing restaurant * Supportive working environment Job Type: Permanent Pay: €29,111\.15\-€53,564\.76 per year Work Location: In person

title: Sous Chef - Barberstown Castle
description: Barberstown Castle 4 Star hotel are looking for an experienced **Sous Chef** to join their established team. This is a full time salaried position and requires **a minimum of 5 years experience as a Sous Chef** in a similar environment. If you have a passion for culinary excellence and customer service and would like to work in a newly renovated kitchen with high end appliances, we would like to hear from you. **Key Duties include but are not limited to:** * Supporting the Executive Head Chef in the daily operation of the kitchen * Maintaining the highest standards of food quality, presentation and consistency * Assisting with menu development and seasonal menu planning * Ensuring HACCP and food safety standards are strictly adhered to * Supervising and mentoring junior kitchen staff * Assisting with the control of stock, ordering and minimising waste * Ensuring smooth kitchen operations during service * Contributing to a positive and professional kitchen culture * Any other duties as may be assigned. **The Must Requirements to Be Considered** * Must have **a minimum of 5 years experience** as a Sous Chef in a similar environment. * Must be living in Ireland and have the right to work in the Republic of Ireland **Salary \& Benefits** * Salary: From €45,000 per annum (negotiable depending on experience) * A dynamic and supportive working environment * Employee Discounts * Tips If you feel that you would like to bring your passion and commitment to Barberstown Castle's Culinary Team, we want to hear from you. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * Food allowance * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Straffan, County Kildare: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Sous Chef: 5 years (required) Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Test Automation Engineer
description: Do you want to join an exciting, growing team that wants to hear your ideas? A team that wants you to be up to date on the latest technologies. Working in BearingPoint you will be part of a team that is passionate about building innovative, high\-quality software for its clients. You will be working in agile teams with all the support you need. BearingPoint is growing quickly, and we are currently hiring junior automation testers to join our QA team. **What skills and experience should you have?** * Passionate about technology, testing and delivering top quality software products. * At least 5 years’ experience in test automation. * Practical experience of test automation including tooling selection and test framework design. * Hands\-on technical experience of building both API and UI test automation frameworks in a range of languages and technologies such as jUnit, Selenium, Serenity BDD (Cucumber, Rest Assured) Robot Framework and JMeter. * Implementation, maintenance and execution of automated test suites to ensure thorough testing of requirements in a fast\-paced Agile environment with an emphasis on BDD and BDD automated tools. * Disciplined reporting, tracking, and resolution of product defects. Implementation of reporting technologies, integrated with automated test runs. * Implementation and management of automated test scripts in CI/CD pipelines. * Experience carrying out code reviews and adhering to best practice guidelines. * Clear understanding of defect management lifecycle. * Hands on knowledge of SQL. * Knowledge of scripting languages (Shell, Perl, Python etc.). * Excellent verbal and written communication skills who excels when working in a multi\-disciplinary agile team. **Why you will enjoy working at BearingPoint Ireland?** We work in an open and collaborative way, where new ideas are always welcome. BearingPoint is an environment where potential and high performance is recognised and rewarded. **What we offer:** You will receive a competitive salary where you are rewarded for both your individual performance and company performance. You will also receive a best\-in\-class benefits package including holidays, private health insurance, pension contributions, gym/club subscription, wellness programmes, mobile phone and much more. In addition, we offer: **Hybrid working –** where each team has autonomy to determine their in\-person and remote working schedule. **Continuous learning –** we understand that it is imperative to invest in our people’s development and we provide external training at prestigious universities such as Oxford and Yale **Social events –** our people perform best when they have a good work life balance, monthly events for all to attend **Diversity** – we are committed to having a diverse workforce where each individual feels they belong and can thrive in a work environment with a strong sense of community **Mentoring \& coaching –** every employee has a development manager to help them grow their career with feedback via continuous conversations **Giving Back –** you will have an opportunity to join our many giving back initiatives including our commitment to sustainability. Our Emissions Calculator gathers accurate emissions data and enables our clients to understand their footprint and track progress on emission reduction and pave the way for a better future. **Together, we are more than business.** To see more from our people with their experiences of BearingPoint please visit our careers website www.bearingpoint.com **About BearingPoint** BearingPoint is an independent management and technology consultancy with European roots and a global reach. The company operates in four units: Consulting, Solutions, Business Services, and Ventures. Consulting covers the advisory business; Solutions provides the tools for successful digital transformation, advanced analytics and regulatory requirements; Business Services provides managed services beyond SaaS; Ventures drives the financing and development of start\-ups. BearingPoint’s clients include many of the world’s leading companies and organizations. The firm has a global consulting network with more than 10,000 people and supports clients in over 75 countries, engaging with them to achieve measurable and sustainable success. BearingPoint is an equal opportunities employer. title: Trainee Hairdresser
description: Alan Keville for Hair are looking for a trainee hairdresser to join our busy team of award winning stylists in the heart of Dublin City Centre. This is a full\-time role, split over five days between Monday \& Saturday. Trainees will start their journey in a supportive capacity, with full professional training provided for those who wish to progress to working stylists. **Duties will include:** \- Assisting stylists at the basin and with finishing services where required. \- Assisting reception with customer service i.e. welcoming \& seating clients, scheduling appointments, co\-ordinating refreshments etc. \- Maintaining cleanliness of salon and ensuring smooth day to day operation. \- Any other ad hoc duties that may arise. No prior salon experience is required, we are simply looking for enthusiastic individuals who are passionate about the industry. The successful candidate will spend the first couple of months familiarising themselves with the salon environment and getting up to speed with the above listed duties, after which they will have regular one\-to\-one training sessions with Alan himself across a variety of disciplines, including basin work, cutting, colour and finishing services. **Benefits include:** \- 4 weeks paid annual leave plus bank holidays. \- Discount on products \& services. \- Team building events. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Trainee Landscape Gardener (Full time)
description: **Trainee Gardener:** We need trainee gardeners for landscape construction and maintenance roles. Based in Dublin for both public and private gardens. Do you enjoy being creative with your hands? Do you have a passion for building gardens? If so, you might be interested in joining our company. We pride ourselves in delivering high quality workmanship and producing innovative green spaces both in public and private sector. MCD Landscapes needs trainee landscapers to join our teams building gardens of all sizes. Our aim is to provide our clients with a high level of satisfaction. You would enjoy and be an integral part of creating gardens and parks using a variety of different materials. With our experience over the years and thousands of completed projects, the company is highly regarded for its professional garden design, construction and maintenance. We want to remain as a premier garden landscaping company for the greater Dublin areas. **Required Skills (some experience or exposure to the following is essential):** · Experience not essential but preferred · Driving licence essential · Trailer licence an advantage but not essential **Benefits:** * Full Time Position * Agreed hourly rate * Overtime rate * Personal development plan/Training \& Development opportunities * Immediate Start **Contact details:** Please email your C.V. to info@mcdlandscapes.iealong with a brief note why you feel you would be an asset to our team. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €15\.00\-€16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company pension * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Landscape: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Trainee Merchandiser
description: Because your perspective shapes trends. Innovate, your way. **Merchandising at Primark** Our merchandising team turn strategic goals into vast opportunities. Here, your insights will maximise our growth as a business and ensure we continue to stay at the forefront in the world of retail fashion. **What You’ll Do As a Trainee Merchandiser** As a Trainee Merchandiser at Primark, you’ll assist the Merchandiser and Assistant Merchandiser in the delivery of our sales, stocks and contribution budgets. You’ll learn about the industry and be given all the tools you need to progress in a Merchandiser role. Primark is a growing business in a competitive industry, with exciting expansion across the UK, Europe, and the US. Your contribution will focus on delivering budgets and line projections, investigating data inaccuracies and forecasting sales. You’ll also have exciting responsibilities relating to products and trends. **What You'll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. * Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish, and a subsidised cafeteria. * Primark Perks – Discounts with some local partner business to our offices **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Analytical \- An analytical thinker with an eye for detail and numbers, who also loves working in a team. * Curious \- Investigate and resolve data inaccuracies, report on stock overages and shortages and ensure that current commitment records have been maintained accurately. * Customer focused \- Develop a strong knowledge of our global customer base and use this knowledge to offer outstanding value to our customer, and key recommendations to your team. * Strategically minded \- Act as a self\-starter, working on a range of products trading in multiple regions. As your knowledge of your product and customer grows, you’ll identify opportunities – learning from sales reactions. * Never miss an opportunity \- Use your initiative to identify current and future trading opportunities and propose your recommendations to your team. * Total organisation \- Use your passion for people to build key relationships that will enable you to progress deliveries and shipping documentation, drive stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with your team. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility requests, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. \#Hybrid Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** REF3150H

title: Treasury Analyst (EMEA)
description: SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of enabling human life on Mars. **TREASURY ANALYST (EMEA)** SpaceX is looking for a Treasury Analyst to join our team to ensure that our treasury capabilities meet the increasing needs of our rapidly growing global Starlink business. This position will support various aspects of treasury operations, including new\-country expansions, cash management, FX strategy and execution, fixed\-income investments, payments, and systems. **Responsibilities** * Build and maintain the company’s expanding worldwide banking network * Research and resolve inquiries regarding global cash activities * Support in the execution of foreign exchange trades required to fund SpaceX's global business * Perform risk analyses related to foreign exchange and other risks * Work with banking partners and FX liquidity providers to explore new systems and methodologies for executing trades in restricted currencies * Investigate opportunities to enhance and standardize processes related to FX trading/reporting and fixed income investment trading/reporting * Execute best practices/controls across all treasury functions * Perform reconciliations and build forecasting mechanisms as needed * Work closely with the global treasury team in the US, Singapore and Ireland to design and build risk monitoring frameworks, systems, and reporting dashboards that present risks and mitigation plans or alternatives **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, economics, business or other business discipline * 2\+ years of corporate treasury or experience in a multinational corporate treasury function or global bank **Preferred Skills And Experience** * Excellent communication (written and verbal), analytical and interpersonal skills * Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks under tight deadlines * Comfortable with Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint * Experience with payments/cash transfers and related procedures, controls and systems * Experience with corporate treasury FX trading platforms such as FXAll, 360T, and Bloomberg FXGO * Familiarity with treasury management systems (e.g. Kyriba, SAP, FIS) and online banking platforms **Additional Requirements** * This position is based in Dublin and requires being onsite \- remote work is not considered * Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Trust & Safety New Associate - Estonian
description: **Position:** Trust and Safety New Associate \- Estonian \- CL13 **Location:** Dublin, Ireland \- Eastpoint D3 **Contract**: Full\-time 40 hours per week, 12 month contract (with view to extension) **Work Hours**: Must be flexible to work in a 24/7 environment. Accenture is a trusted, innovative, comprehensive, and experienced partner to leading platform companies. The Trust and Safety offering within Accenture Operations helps keep the internet safe and helps platform companies accelerate, scale, and improve their businesses.. Content moderators serve as an important line of defense by helping improve the quality and safety of online content while gaining hands\-on job experience. You will deliver value to our clients, while making a larger impact on society. You will be responsible for analyzing and reviewing user profiles, audio, videos, and text\-based content and/or investigating, escalating and/or resolving issues that are reported by users or flagged by the system. Due to the nature of the , the individual may be exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns. Content moderation is meaningful work that helps keep the internet safe. It may also be challenging, at times. In the context of this , individuals may be directly or inadvertently exposed to potentially objectionable and sensitive content (e.g., graphic, violent, sexual, or egregious). Therefore, content moderators need strong resilience and coping skills. We care for the health and well\-being of our people and provide the support and resources needed to perform their responsibilities. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being. **Details** * Review, classify and/or remove content according to client guidelines, using specific tools and channels * Understand and remain updated on changing client policies and guidelines * Investigate, resolve, and relay complex content issues to the broader Trust and Safety team * Serve as an advocate for the user community * Participate in process improvement initiatives that improve quality and efficiency of work * Participate in continuous training programs and workgroup discussions for optimal development in the * Engage in conversation around socially sensitive topics with the purpose of keeping our communities safe **Basic Qualifications/Skills Needed** * Strong coping, emotional resilience, and stress\-management skills * Excellent comprehension, communication, and English language skills * Ability to perform duties objectively, devoid of inherent biases or personal beliefs, in the best interest of client policies * Strong attention to detail * Comfort synthesizing and analyzing information from multiple streams * Strong critical thinking and decision\-making skills * Deep familiarity and passion for the internet, internet platforms, and internet culture * High affinity and cultural awareness of political and social situations regarding the relevant market and region that will be supported * Ability to work well individually and as part of a team * Ability to work differing rotations/shifts and non\-standard work hours * Flexibility in meeting changing business needs in a fast\-paced environment * Commitment to quality, efficiency, and effectiveness * Previous business process outsourcing, customer service, or content moderation experience is a plus * Over 18 years of age * Shift timings/requirements to work weekends and holidays **Benefits:** * Working for an international enterprise company * Great training * Company social events * Opportunities for carrier progression Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity. If you are required to work a night shift as part of this role, you are obliged to complete a night worker health assessment. Please note that it is a requirement of your continued employment, that you are fit to work night shifts. **About Accenture** Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Interactive, Technology and Operations services — all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. Our 674,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at accenture.com. **Disclaimer** Content moderation is meaningful work that may be challenging at times. In the context of this role, individuals may directly or inadvertently be exposed to potentially objectionable and sensitive content and will need strong resilience and coping skills. We care for the health and well\-being of our people and provide the support and resources needed to execute the responsibilities of their roles. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being. Due to the nature of the role, the user may be frequently exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns. **INCLUSION AND DIVERSITY** We are a global collective of diverse talents. Innovators improving the way the world works and lives. We provide an accessible, inclusive, open and welcoming workplace that helps everyone make the most of their professional potential. Discover how we are accelerating equality for all in the workplace. Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com. **As part of the interview process, you will be required to complete an English language test and a language test in the relevant language for this role. You will be advised of the level you are required to achieve to successfully carry out the role.** **\#LI\-EU**

title: Valuation Surveyors (All levels)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** We are growing our market‑leading Valuation \& Advisory division and are seeking ambitious Chartered Valuation Surveyors at all levels — from graduate surveyor/newly qualified surveyors ready to build their expertise, to senior leaders seeking a platform to develop and mentor teams and drive client relationships. Apply now, if you want to work with a firm that values technical excellence, supports professional growth, and gives you exposure to some of Ireland’s most significant assets and clients. **Key Responsibilities** ***Newly Qualified Surveyors/Graduates*** * Assist in preparing high\-quality RICS Red Book valuation and advisory reports for banks, funds, developers, and corporate clients * Conduct inspections, market research, and comparable analysis * Support senior colleagues on complex instructions * Build strong client relationships and develop commercial awareness * Contribute to pitches, proposals, and team initiatives ***Senior up to Director\-Level Surveyors*** * Lead valuation instructions and manage key client relationships * Oversee and review valuation reports to ensure accuracy and compliance * Engage in Landlord \& Tenant matters, including rent reviews and lease renewals. * Contribute to the periodic review of team processes and procedures. * Mentor and support junior surveyors * Provide expert advice on complex assets, portfolios, and development appraisals * Contribute to shared knowledge within the team and the wider group. * Generate and win new business through networking and client engagement. **Requirements** * MRICS/MSCSI \- VRS Registered Valuer * Strong Technical Knowledge of valuation methodologies including Discounted Cash Flows. * Knowledge of Argus Enterprise and Developer Valuation packages. * Professional presentation with good customer service and communication skills required. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills \& attention to detail is essential. * Strong time management and organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Team player with strong interpersonal skills Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.

title: Veterinary Nurse - Graduate / Junior Position
description: **Veterinary Nurse – Graduate / Junior Position** **Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic, Dublin Full\-time, Permanent Salary €30,000–€32,000 DOE Start date: May 2026 (flexible)** **A Supportive Start to Your Nursing Career** Starting your career as a Veterinary Nurse is a big step, and finding the right environment makes all the difference. At Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic, we offer a genuinely supportive, close\-knit team where you’ll be guided, encouraged and given the time to grow in confidence. This is a fantastic opportunity for a graduate or junior nurse to join a busy small animal clinic that combines excellent general practice with specialist endoscopy services. You’ll gain exposure to a varied caseload while building strong, practical skills in a setting that never feels overwhelming. **Learn in a Small, Collaborative Team** You’ll be working as part of a small, friendly team made up of two vets, two nurses and two animal care assistants. As one half of a two\-nurse team, you’ll play a key role in the day\-to\-day running of the clinic while always having support close by. This environment allows you to get properly involved, ask questions, and learn by doing. You’ll be supported in developing your skills across all areas of nursing, with particular opportunities to get involved in surgical work and endoscopy as your confidence grows. **A Unique Clinical Environment** Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic is a modern, 100% small animal practice with a special interest in endoscopy. This gives you the chance to be part of more advanced procedures while still building strong foundations in general practice nursing. The clinic is ideally located just a short walk from Cherrywood Luas and close to Loughlinstown Hospital, making it easily accessible while being part of a great local community. **A Working Pattern That Supports You** This is a full\-time role based on a 40\-hour week, with no weekends, no out\-of\-hours and no bank holiday requirements. It’s a rare opportunity to achieve a genuinely healthy work\-life balance from the very start of your career, while still gaining valuable clinical experience. **Who This Role Is For** This position is open to graduate or junior nurses, and prior experience is not essential. What matters most is your attitude and willingness to learn. You’ll need to be VCI registered, or due to join the register in 2026\. Beyond that, we’re looking for someone who is motivated, proactive, and takes pride in delivering high\-quality patient care. If you enjoy being part of a team, show initiative in your work, and want to build a strong foundation for your future career, you’ll fit in well here. **Ongoing Support and Development** We understand how important those first few years in practice are, which is why you’ll have access to structured support through our Graduate Nurse Academy. This includes mentoring, learning resources and guidance to help you develop at the right pace. You’ll also have access to funded CPD, further training opportunities, and the option to pursue additional qualifications as your interests develop. **Benefits – Subject To Pro Rata** We recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our staff, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay **Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Annual pay reviews * Pawesome benefits – various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Relocation packages * Free flu jabs * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurse Committee, Clinical Board and many more) **A Place to Grow** Cherrywood Veterinary Clinic offers more than just your first job. It’s a place where you’ll feel supported, valued and part of a team from day one. If you’re looking for a positive, welcoming environment where you can build confidence, develop your skills and enjoy coming to work, we’d love to hear from you. €30,000 \- €32,000 a year Up to €32,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Veterinary Surgeon – Small Animal (GP Focus)
description: **Vetcare Athy, Co. Kildare Full\-time, Fixed Term Contract (Part\-time considered) Salary up to €70,000 DOE (FTE) Start date from May 2026 (flexible)** **A Solid GP Role in a Well\-Run, Supportive Practice** Vetcare Athy is seeking a confident, well\-rounded small animal Veterinary Surgeon to join the team on a maternity cover contract. This role is ideal for an experienced GP vet who enjoys the rhythm of first opinion practice, is comfortable managing a varied caseload, and takes pride in delivering consistent, high\-quality care. You’ll be joining a busy but well\-organised clinic where the focus is on doing the fundamentals well. This is a role for someone who values good clinical standards, enjoys building client relationships, and is confident across routine consults, surgery and day\-to\-day case management. **The Role – Confident First Opinion Practice** This is a full\-time position working 42 hours per week, Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm, with a 1 in 4 Saturday rota from 9:00am to 3:00pm. You’ll be managing a broad and steady caseload, including consultations, routine surgeries, dentistry and medical work\-ups. The role will suit someone who is comfortable with the pace of general practice and capable of working independently when needed, including periods of sole charge. There are no out\-of\-hours requirements, no Sundays and no bank holiday work, giving you a predictable schedule and a good balance outside of work. **A Practice That Supports Good Clinical Work** Vetcare Athy is the flagship clinic within the Vetcare group, offering a well\-equipped and professionally run environment. While the focus remains on first opinion care, the practice also supports more complex case work\-ups and procedures, giving you the option to expand your clinical exposure if that’s of interest. Facilities include digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory, allowing you to work cases to a high standard. The team is experienced, approachable and collaborative, creating an environment where you can focus on doing your job well without unnecessary pressure. **Flexible Options to Suit You** While this is advertised as a full\-time position, we are very open to hearing from experienced vets seeking part\-time or alternative working patterns. If you’re looking for something that better fits around other commitments, let us know what works for you and we’ll do our best to accommodate where possible. **Who This Role Will Suit** This position is best suited to an experienced Veterinary Surgeon who is confident in routine small animal practice. You’ll be comfortable with consultations, diagnostics, routine soft tissue surgery and dentistry, and happy managing cases from start to finish. You’ll also be someone who works well within a team, communicates clearly, and takes a practical, steady approach to clinical work. This is not a role for a new graduate, but it is ideal for a GP vet who enjoys the variety and consistency of first opinion practice and wants to work in a well\-supported setting. **Part of a Wider, Supportive Network** As part of the Vetcare group and IVC Evidensia, you’ll have access to a wider clinical network, resources and potential future opportunities. While this is a fixed\-term contract, it can be a great way to build relationships within the group and explore longer\-term options if they arise. **A Package That Reflects Your Experience** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing and professional development. This includes generous annual leave, paid CPD, professional memberships, wellbeing supports, pension contributions and a range of additional benefits. **A Straightforward, High\-Quality GP Role** If you’re looking for a role where you can step in, feel confident in your work, and be part of a supportive, well\-run practice, this could be an excellent fit. We’d love to hear from you. €0 \- €70,000 a year Up to €70,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

The team is experienced, approachable and collaborative, creating an environment where you can focus on doing your job well without unnecessary pressure. **Flexible Options to Suit You** -------------------------------- While this is advertised as a full\-time position, we are very open to hearing from experienced vets seeking part\-time or alternative working patterns. If you’re looking for something that better fits around other commitments, let us know what works for you and we’ll do our best to accommodate where possible. **Who This Role Will Suit** --------------------------- This position is best suited to an experienced Veterinary Surgeon who is confident in routine small animal practice. You’ll be comfortable with consultations, diagnostics, routine soft tissue surgery and dentistry, and happy managing cases from start to finish. You’ll also be someone who works well within a team, communicates clearly, and takes a practical, steady approach to clinical work. This is not a role for a new graduate, but it is ideal for a GP vet who enjoys the variety and consistency of first opinion practice and wants to work in a well\-supported setting. **Part of a Wider, Supportive Network** --------------------------------------- As part of the Vetcare group and IVC Evidensia, you’ll have access to a wider clinical network, resources and potential future opportunities. While this is a fixed\-term contract, it can be a great way to build relationships within the group and explore longer\-term options if they arise. **A Package That Reflects Your Experience** ------------------------------------------- Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing and professional development. This includes generous annual leave, paid CPD, professional memberships, wellbeing supports, pension contributions and a range of additional benefits. **A Straightforward, High\-Quality GP Role** -------------------------------------------- If you’re looking for a role where you can step in, feel confident in your work, and be part of a supportive, well\-run practice, this could be an excellent fit. We’d love to hear from you.
